observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:

Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of the physical count of all PPE and prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following year;

GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare, maintain and update the Property Cards (PC) and PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) for all PPE accounts; and

Municipal Accountant to submit the complete subsidiary ledgers of all PPE accounts to support the balance recorded in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor to submit the lacking documents on the acquisition of two lots with a Memorandum of Encumbrance from the Department of Agrarian Reform;
Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account Other Prepayments amounting to ₱6.439 million to the Land account for the three lots purchased and record the Land and the corresponding liability/payable account for the unrecognized portion amounting to ₱5.079 million; and
Inventory and Appraisal Committee to accurately identify all the lands owned by the Municipality and provide proper valuation thereof to correctly record the Land account in the books.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to designate a responsible person to facilitate the processing of the Certificates of Title of the 19 parcels of land and other donated lots not yet transferred to the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership of the Municipality over those properties.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare and submit the subsidiary ledgers per fund transfer/purpose of the accounts Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs for easy monitoring and validation of the balances reported in the financial statements; and

Determine the composition of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts and make necessary adjustments to reflect the actual unliquidated balances, and to, henceforth, debit only the same accounts upon liquidation of the fund transfers to the respective source agencies.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:

Municipal Accountant to revert to the National Treasury the unutilized balance of the Odette Fund amounting to ₱989,739.00; 

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the DSWD personnel-in-charge concerning the stale check which was issued as refund of unutilized fund; and

Concerned implementing offices to expedite the implementation of programs/activities to utilize the fund transfers received within the prescribed period and strictly observe the guidelines set in the Memoranda of Agreement and/or prescribed guidelines.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to: 

Direct the personnel concerned to refund the amount paid as membership fee in a private organization;
 
Refrain from allowing membership fees of employees of the Municipality to private organizations to be charged against the government funds; and 

Henceforth, adhere to the provision of DBM-NBC No. 563 dated April 22, 2016 on the claim of Training expenses.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS within ten (10) days after receipt of the bank statements and submit the same monthly to the Auditor, pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011; and

Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify the details of the Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Savings Account and the two Trust Fund accounts recorded in the books but were not included in the Bank Confirmation Reply.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the:

GSO-Designate ensure that issuances of inventory are supported with Requisition and Issue Slips, and prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued to the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and

Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories semestrally to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories that must be submitted to the COA Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that the recognition of the expenses relative to the issuance of inventories are duly supported with Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued accompanied by Requisition and Issue Slips.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the transaction on the agency books based on the complete Inventory Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to verify and correct the possible duplication of records of land, especially those barangays adjacent to the Province of Capiz and neighboring towns.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce the collection of the delinquent market stall rentals by imposing the sanctions provided in the Contract of Lease.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO-Designate and other concerned municipal officials to determine the priority PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF and ensure its full implementation in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in order to address and strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to verify the excess disbursements on travelling and training expenses over the appropriations and submit computation/justification to support the disbursements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor submit a justification why the Municipality did not resort to the procurement of its food, venue and accommodation during the conduct of the Executive-Legislative Agenda (ELA) and Capacity Development (CAPDEV) Plan workshop conducted on August 22-24, 2022, and use the OR of AMPDCI for the payment of registration fee amounting to ₱13,000.00.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Submit the lacking supporting documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of the payment of honoraria to resource persons; and

Submit computation of honoraria of each resource person based on the formula provided in DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2007-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

xxx

b) Direct the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to accurately identify all the lands owned by the Municipality and provide proper valuation thereof to correctly record the Land account in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor propose to the Sanggunian the creation of the mandatory positions in the MDRRMO and to provide funds thereof in accordance with the guidelines and provisions set by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a justification why there is a change in the intended purpose of the fund and seek approval from the Iloilo Provincial Government to rectify the situation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the concerned Municipal officials to follow the guidelines and procedures set in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the write-off, if warranted, of the unliquidated cash advances which have been long outstanding/remained dormant in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned municipal officials and employees to submit the lacking documents and to ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enforce COE Nos. 2016-083 and 2016-084, by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the audit disallowances. 

We also recommended that in case disallowances shall be settled by installment payments, the persons liable request from the COA for approval to allow them to pay such on installment basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review/analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.    95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to refund their excess claims considering the requirement that any amount in excess of the prescribed rate shall be at the expense of the participants.
We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the CY 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,341,420.00.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to the following:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive annual physical inventory of its properties and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs Volume 1;
Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to prepare and submit their respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards using the prescribed formats; and
Revisit the computations of Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation, as well as the allocation of salvage values to some of the PPE items to ensure the accuracy of their net book values.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to consider the immediate return of the unutilized cash donations received, totaling to ₱0.873 million, to the source agencies since the relief and recovery needs brought about by those typhoons have long been addressed.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the MDRRMO-designate to prepare and submit regularly the following to the Office of the Auditor (a) Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (b) LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format and content pursuant to the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor to submit the lacking documents on the acquisition of two lots that were intended as site for quarantine facilities such as the Certificate Authorizing Registration from BIR and the proof of payments of Capital Gains Tax, Documentary Stamp Tax and Transfer Tax; and
Make representation with the Provincial Government of Iloilo and request for the appraisal of the lots whose individual book values could not be determined to ensure their correct recording in the books of accounts and facilitate their proper recording in case that some of these will be donated to public schools within the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly prepare the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circulars 2016-004 and 2012-002 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures in order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MDRRMO-Designate to properly plan, give emphasis and prioritize the timely implementation of LDRRM PPAs identified in the LDRRMFIP in order to address and strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan concerning the enactment of an appropriate ordinance covering the Solid Waste Management revenue-generating activities.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MENRO-Designate, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned officers and employees to ensure that collections derived from revenue-generating activities, particularly in Solid Waste Management, are duly receipted and deposited in the account of the Municipality and disbursements related thereto conform with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to issue a justification as to the non-conduct of post-qualification to the Lowest Calculated Bidder (LCB) before recommending to the HoPE the award of the contract; and

Ensure that the BAC has complied with all the requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its 2016 revised IRR before approving the recommendation of award.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC and all concerned officials and employees to adhere to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its 2016 revised IRR in conducting its procurement activities.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:

Concerned officers and employees to stop the practice of reimbursement or direct purchase of goods and services; and

Bids and Award Committee to submit the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the Municipality every year to the Audit Team.

Program Coordinator to provide a conservative estimate of the number of attendees/participants in the program or activity to be implemented and conduct the proper procurement process of meals and snacks prior to the commencement of the said program or activity. 

Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed thru the Bids and Awards Committee, to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR in conducting its procurement activities.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:

GSO-Designate to ensure complete delivery of goods and services procured within the schedule stipulated in the Purchase Orders or Contracts; and"

Municipal Accountant to strictly impose liquidated damages against payments to suppliers who incurred delays in conformity with the provisions of the contracts, and cause the collection of the liquidated damages amounting to ₱32,566.44 by demanding its payment from the supplier, or by deducting the same from any money due to or which may become due to the supplier concerned.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

Discontinue the granting of Productivity Incentive Allowance/Benefit and communication allowance to its officers and employees; and

Release the payment of Cash Gift not earlier than November 15 of the current year as provided in DBM Budget Circular No. 2016-4 dated April 28, 2016.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan agreed to ensure that the payment of allowances, incentives, and other benefits to its officers and employees are expressly provided by law, and other statutory authority, and the rules and regulations promulgated by competent authority to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures of government funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and HRMO to initiate the establishment of policy and guidelines in the selection and recruitment of Job Order and Contract of Service personnel.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO, together with the Municipal Development Council, to review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include a list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years and incorporate it in the AIP and prepare the SIP for the newly identified PPAs.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that all travel claims are duly authorized by proper officials and supported with complete documentation prior to the approval of disbursements and upon liquidation, in case of cash advances.

We likewise recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality prepare and submit the duly accomplished AAPSI with supporting documents to the Auditor on the prescribed timeline, and likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a copy of the said report and ensure that said status report is posted in the Municipality’s website.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor assign/ designate a personnel or team to accept and distribute donation in behalf of the Municipality and to prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with the required supporting documents and submit the same to the Audit Team within 10 working days after the end of each quarter with proof of submission to the National Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Council (NDRRMC), through the OCD, to properly account donations received in cash or in kind.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization for monitoring, transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

a)	direct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of the physical count of all PPE and prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following year;

b)	require the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare, maintain and update the Property Cards (PC) and PPE Ledger Cards for all PPE accounts; and

c)	direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete subsidiary ledgers of all PPE accounts to support the balance recorded in the financial statements.


We further recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

xxx

Direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the specific details of the undepreciated PPEs and recognize depreciation expense for all depreciable PPE items.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a)	Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the cashbooks and accounting records regularly so that cash account balances are accurately presented in the financial statements;

b)	OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS within ten (10) days after receipt of the bank statements and submit the same monthly to the Auditor, pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011; and

c)	Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify the details of the Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Savings Account and the two Trust Fund accounts recorded in the books but were not included in the Bank Confirmation Reply.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the:

a)	documentary evidence, including the MOA, Special project guidelines, SARO/credit memos and other proof of fund transfer, proof of return of excess or unutilized fund, and other documents peculiar to the fund transfer; and 

b)	subsidiary ledgers of the accounts Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs under the Trust Fund as of December 31, 2022.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that the liquidation report of the fund transfers with all the originals of vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the:

a)	GSO-Designate ensure that issuances of inventory are supported with Requisition and Issue Slips, and prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued to the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and

b)	Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories semestrally to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories that must be submitted to the COA Office not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. 

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that the recognition of the expenses relative to the issuance of inventories are duly supported with Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued accompanied by Requisition and Issue Slips.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:


a)	GSO-Designate and Municipal Health Officer to ensure that all donated           COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies received be properly accounted and the corresponding Inventory Reports thereof be prepared and submitted to the Accounting Office for recording in the books; and

b)	OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the transaction on the agency books based on the complete Inventory Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting a more aggressive tax campaign and by strictly implementing the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of R.A. No. 7160.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to verify and correct the possible duplication of records of land, especially those barangays adjacent to the Province of Capiz and neighboring towns.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO-Designate and other concerned municipal officials to determine the priority PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF and ensure its full implementation in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in order to address and strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

b)	The OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that the PPAs are included in the AIP prior to the processing of disbursements related to its implementation; and


c)	The OIC-Municipal Accountant coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant in the preparation and submission of the Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and the Subsidiary Ledgers for transfers made to the Special Trust Fund by year of transfer for CY 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly prepare the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circulars 2016-004 and 2012-002 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures in order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Vice Mayor, and the Sangguniang Bayan Members and its employees shall, henceforth, secure first the approval, signature and/certification of the proper authorities with regard to their official travels pursuant to Executive Order No. 77 and Republic Act No. 7160.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that all travel claims are duly authorized by proper officials and supported with complete documentation prior to the approval of disbursements and upon liquidation, in case of cash advances.

submit computation of honoraria of each resource person based on the formula provided in DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2007-1; and

henceforth, adhere to the provisions of DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the grant of honoraria to resource persons.

The MDRRMO, together with the Municipal Development Council, to review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include a list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years and incorporate it in the AIP and prepare the SIP for the newly identified PPAs;

submit the lacking supporting documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of the payment of honoraria to resource persons;

Designate a responsible person to facilitate the processing of the Certificate of Title of the 14 parcels of land and those other lots donated to the Municipality that were not yet transferred in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality over those properties; and
Direct the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to accurately identify all the lands owned by the Municipality and provide proper valuation thereof to correctly record the Land account in the books.

Recommendation

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inspection and Appraisal Committee to provide the appraised value of the unserviceable PPEs identified and facilitate the disposal thereof in accordance with the procedures provided by the aforementioned guidelines.

Recommendations

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and the Bids and Awards Committee issue a proper delegation through a resolution to the personnel assigned to purchase POL products and ensure that proper procurement procedure is followed in compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that driver’s trip tickets be properly accomplished and serially numbered in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 to properly monitor and ascertain the propriety of fuel consumption of the Municipality.

Recommendations

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other personnel responsible to strictly adhere with the aforementioned provisions by submitting the following documents:
Daily Time Record;
List of frontline HRHs with their assigned workstations and corresponding official duties and responsibilities; and
Certification by the head of hospital, laboratory, or medical and quarantine facilities, or his/her authorized representative that the frontline HRHs physically report for work for the period covered.

Recommendation

We reiterated in our prior years’ recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor thru the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR, and pertinent guidelines provided in GPPB Resolutions No. 03-2020 and 05-2020 to efficiently conduct procurement activities, specifically during state of calamity.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor propose to the Sanggunian the creation of the mandatory positions in the MDRRMO and to provide funds thereof in accordance with the guidelines and provisions set by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

Recommendation

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor assign/designate a personnel or team to accept and distribute donation in behalf of the Municipality and to prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with the required supporting documents and submit the same to the Audit Team within 10 working days after the end of each quarter with proof of submission to the National Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Council (NDRRMC), through the OCD, to properly account donations received in cash or in kind.

Recommendation

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer process the application for Fidelity bond of the those unbonded Accountable Officers pursuant to the provisions of P.D. No. 1445, R.A. No. 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to safeguard government funds and properties from to risk of non-recovery due to loss, improper or unauthorized use and misappropriation thereof.

Recommendation

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct all the concerned Department Heads to formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit immediately the AAPSI form to the audit team, and likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a copy of the said report and ensure that said status report is posted in the Municipality’s website.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the origin and nature of the discrepancies in the Cash in Local Treasury account both for the General and Special Education Fund; and immediately pass the necessary journal entry to reconcile their records. Reconciliation of the cashbook and SL/GL balances shall be conducted at least quarterly in order to ensure completeness of the recorded financial transactions and accuracy of the Cash in Local Treasury account balance as of a certain period.

observations.recommendation:
For the above observations, the Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Supply Officer thru the Municipal General Services Officer to:
i. immediately prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with the corresponding Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for inventory items already consumed and identified as distributed to the implementing offices and end-users, particularly the RIS for the items issued which were taken out from Other Supplies and Materials Inventory (GF), Agricultural and Marine Supplies Inventory (GF), Other Supplies and Materials Inventory (GF) and Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution (SEF) in the amount of P8,270,694.75, P59,800.00, P623,926.60 and P197,459.00, respectively, in order to establish accurate balances of affected inventory accounts;
ii. prospectively, completely document the issuance and distribution of supplies and materials with the use of properly filled-out RIS. Thereafter, prepare the monthly SSMI duly supported by RIS and submit the same to the accounting office in order to establish accurate balances;
iii. prepare and maintain complete stock cards for each item of inventories to account for all receipts and issuances so as to determine accurate balances on hand at a given period, and ensure that the ending balances are tally with that of the general ledger; and
iv. incorporate full disclosure of information about the inventory accounts and their balances in the notes to financial statements.
b. Inventory Committee thru the Municipal General Service Officer to:
i. submit a valid explanation on why the required RPCI has not been submitted for several years; and
ii. conduct the complete periodic physical inventory of unissued inventory items by type, consolidate the results in the required format of RPCI and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Ensure that the results of inventory count as incorporated in the RPCI is reconciled with the records of the accounting office.
c. Municipal Accountant to:
i. make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect accurate balances of inventory accounts with overstatement of P15,444,994.91 and corresponding expenses with understatement for the same amount as at year-end;
ii. stop the practice of recording the procurement of supplies and materials as outright expenses, instead such shall be first coursed through as Inventory and then recognized as an expense upon consumption, issuance or distribution to the implementing offices or end-users;
iii. commence with the preparation of subsidiary ledger cards for each type of inventories to account for all receipts and issuances so as to determine accurate balances on hand at a given period, and ensure that the ending balances are tally with that of the general ledger; and
iv. incorporate full disclosure of information about the inventory accounts and their balances in the notes to financial statements.
d. all concerned requisitioning/implementing offices of the Municipality to:
i. conduct the physical inventory of supplies and materials still in their possession at least once a year and report the results in the RPCI; 
ii. prepare and submit the RPCI in the prescribed format to the accounting office for them to correctly account the balances of inventory accounts, and to the MGSO for them to prepare the consolidated RPCI at the close of the year; and
iii. the MGSO to cause the submission of the consolidated RPCI to the Auditor on or before January 31 every year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all its PPE and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following year; (AAR 2019 Finding No. 11)

b. Require compliance of the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate/ Supply Officer to update and maintain the specific Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PC), respectively for all PPE of the Municipality; and



c. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to reconcile the amounts recorded in the books with the RPCPPE in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 to establish the correctness and reliability of the balances of PPE accounts reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Accountant record the receipt and issuance of inventories under the appropriate inventory and expense accounts;

b. GSO-Designate ensure that issuances of inventory are supported with Requisition and Issue Slips, and to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant; and

c. Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at year-end to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories that must be submitted to the COA Office not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that 20% Development Fund disbursements are within the priority programs and projects identified in Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, dated February 22, 2017 and must be included in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally and/or fully utilized in accordance with the guidelines to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and maximize the benefits for the Municipality’s constituents. (AAR 2019 Finding No. 4)

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant will make the necessary correcting entries to change the entries previously made and ensure that succeeding transactions be properly recorded in the appropriate accounts so as to fairly and accurately present the amounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended and the management agreed that the depreciation expense for the year of the unrecorded Infrastructure Assets identified will be recognized.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor utilize the LDRRMF for PPAs which are consistent with the existing laws, rules, and regulations in order to provide more appropriate disaster response and measures that will directly address the needs of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections intact and in full amount in accordance with the policies and procedures prescribed under P.D. No. 1445 and DOF-BLGF Local Treasury Operations Manual to avoid possible loss or misappropriation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a justification why there is a change in the intended purpose of the fund and seek approval from the Iloilo Provincial Government to rectify the situation.





We also recommended that the Municipality ensure that succeeding financial assistance received be utilized for its specific purpose.

We recommended and the management agreed to submit the copy of identification cards of those beneficiaries determined to have not submitted the same during the distribution for verification and ensure that succeeding distributions be supported with complete documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned municipal officials to observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF and to ensure the full utilization of the Fund according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enforce COE Nos. 2016-083 and 2016-084, by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the audit disallowances. 





We also recommended that in case disallowances shall be settled by installment payments, the persons liable request from the COA for approval to allow them to pay such on installment basis.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide additional accounting staff to assist the Municipal Accountant to prepare the various financial reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review/analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized government depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson, together with the other members of the LDRRM Council, comply with the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage to adequately prepare the Municipality when hazards/ disasters strike.

We also recommended that the LCE provide additional personnel qualified to occupy the three (3) staff positions under the LDRRMO.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials to:

Set-up and maintain a GAD Database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation;



Ensure timely preparation and submission of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report in required format together with necessary documents to the DILG Regional Office VI for review and approval to ascertain compliance with the guidelines; and

Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized by the Municipality according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-Designate to see to it that all purchases of gasoline, fuel and oil are supported with complete documentation, all DTTs are properly accomplished and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 77-61.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to refund their excess claims considering the requirement that any amount in excess of the prescribed rate shall be at the expense of the participants.
We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the CY 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,341,420.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to ensure the full utilization of the allocated budget for plans/programs that will address gender issues identified for the realization of GAD objectives which are Gender Equality and Women Empowerment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the concerned Department Heads/Divisions Chiefs to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2018 AAR recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their department/divisions, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor. 

We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted to the Auditor within sixty (60) days from the receipt of the AAR, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.
Furthermore, we recommended that the LCE provide additional accounting staff to assist the Municipal Accountant to prepare the various financial reports.
Review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and
We also recommended that the LCE provide additional personnel qualified to occupy the three (3) staff positions under the LDRRMO.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization for monitoring, transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents

Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, MDRRMO and BAC to:
a. submit a valid explanation on why the procurement of goods charged against the
₱5.0 million cash donation from the Office of the President failed to pass through the
proper mode of procurement and incurrence of ineligible expenses charged against
the fund;
b. for further evaluation of the Audit Team, cause submission of the lacking
documents supporting the liquidation reports;
c. Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of procuring goods from cash advances,
except for petty cash expenses in accordance with applicable laws, rules and
regulations;
d. BAC to strictly adhere to the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 on the use
of the alternative mode of procurement, if competitive bidding is not viable;
e. MDRRMO, in the event of an emergency or such like disaster, to assess and
prepare the necessary supplemental plan, and thereafter request for the purchase
of the much needed goods/services upon approval of the HoPE and of the Local
Sanggunian, if warranted in order to prevent piecemeal procurement; and
f. Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents supporting the
disbursement vouchers before making any payment.

In view of the foregoing, the Audit Team recommends that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper journal entries for recording
of receipts and disbursements under the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut or
omission of entries for proper monitoring of the balances of the accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. MPDO to ensure optimal utilization of funds for priority projects under the 20
percent DF to enable the Municipality and its constituents to reap the socio-
economic benefits at the earliest possible time as prescribed of Section 5 of the
DILG–DBM JMC No. 2017-1;
b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to refrain from
presenting projects in lump sum, rather, present the PPAs to be funded by the 20
percent DF on a per project and per Barangay basis together with its
corresponding estimated cost for itemized appropriation in the Annual and
Supplemental Budget; and
c. Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements are properly supported with
complete documentation. Discontinue the practice of processing the payment for
claims that are not sufficiently documented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee,
Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. provide a valid explanation as to the insufficiency of documents supporting
claims against government funds, and the full identification of the office and ICT
equipment acquired in lump sum price;
b. submit the lacking documentary requirements for the procurement projects
indicated above for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to cause the allocation of the purchase cost of the
properties acquired in lump sum in order to obtain a proper basis for computing
depreciation, and ensure the completeness of the documents attached to the
disbursement vouchers before making any payment.

Likewise, we recommended that the Local School Board to continuously adhere to the
appropriate laws, rules and regulations on the proper usage of the Special
Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and all concerned
Department Head to:
a. formulate the Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) Annual
Procurement Plan (APP), Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and Agency
Procurement Compliance and Performance Indicator (APCPI) Results in
accordance with the 2016 RIRR and GPPB Circular Nos. 06-2019 and 02-2020;
b. ensure that the APP, PMR and APCPI are prepared in the prescribed format and
that all the necessary information required therein are sufficiently incorporated; and
c. submit the duly approved copy of the procurement reports to the GPPB within the
deadline set for submission.

For transparency and accountability over donated cash and in kind entrusted by the
donors to the Municipality, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
MGSO, MDRRMO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit the lacking documentary requirements and a valid explanation as to non-
compliance to the aforesaid guidelines on reporting of donations in kind; and
b. strictly abide by the basic controls on receipt, recording, distribution and
reporting of donations be it cash or in kind; and c) MGSO and MT to provide
status of the P4,000.00 cash assistance indicated in the list of donations given by
MCG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), Municipal General Services Officer, and the
Municipal Accountant to:
i. submit a valid explanation relative to the insufficiency of information provided
in some documents and the absence of other supporting requirements for claims
against government funds; and
ii. submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team for further
evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents attached
to disbursement vouchers before payment is made; and
c. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the posting and reporting
requirements of the GPPB Circular 01-2020 for information purposes and
transparency, and ensure that all the documents such as but not limited to PRs and
POs are properly filled out with complete information.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Municipal Accountant to
submit the accomplished SAC forms and their supporting documents for
Barangays District III, Matingain I and Talaga, for evaluation of the Audit Team.
Likewise, ensure that the required documents relative to future distributions of
financial assistance are intact and available for audit;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payrolls are
properly signed by claimants in the future SAP payouts; and
c. concerned officials to abide by all deadlines set by the DSWD and their
Memorandum of Agreement so as not to compromise subsequent financial
subsidies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:

a.  The Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties and equipment of the Municipality and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; 

b. The Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain and update the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for proper identification of all PPEs; and

c.  The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with the submitted RPCPPE in order to present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements.

d. The Designated GSO to issue ARE to Municipal officials and employees concerned to cover the equipment issued for proper control and accountability of equipment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all unreleased checks be reported and a Journal Entry Voucher be prepared by the Municipal Accountant to record the entry for the restoration of cash equivalent to the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate liability accounts.

We recommended the following:

a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE), as Chairman, and all the other members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), to prepare the Annual Investment Plan containing priority development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include only specific and identifiable PPAs with corresponding quantifiable targets, attainable within the desired time frame, and consistent with the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1;  Moreover, the MDC and other heads of offices shall regularly monitor the progress of the projects, their timely, effective and efficient implementation, and the commitment from the concerned offices to implement the PPAs as planned; and

b. The LCE to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally and/or fully utilized in accordance with the guidelines to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and maximize the benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned municipal officials to:

a. Observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF and to ensure the full utilization of the Fund according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents; and 



b. Review and revise/update the LDRRMFIP regularly to include the list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years, subject to the approval of the local sanggunian and the LCE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and concerned municipal officials to refrain from conducting seminar, training or similar activity out of town and to give preference to the use of government-owned venues.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 by ensuring that all procurement shall be done in accordance with the prescribed guidelines so that most advantageous terms, conditions and prices are obtained by the government. 

Furthermore, we recommended that the LCE require the concerned municipal officials and employees to submit the lacking documents and to ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENRO-Designate and other members of the Solid Waste Management Board to ensure that proper revisions be made to the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enforce COE Nos. 2016-083 and 2016-084, by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the audit disallowances. 





We also recommended that in case disallowances shall be settled by installment payments, the persons liable request from the COA for approval to allow them to pay such on installment basis. 

We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Acting Municipal Treasurer in the preparation and submission of an updated schedule of unsettled disallowances of all persons liable with details of the settlements and supporting documents for issuance of NSSDC/s and reconciliation of accounting records with the issued SASDC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct all concerned municipal officials to prioritize the conduct of the physical inventory/count of properties to ascertain their existence and condition.

We also recommended that the LCE coordinate with the Municipal Vice Mayor, who is the former Municipal Mayor, to comply with the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2019-39 for the official turnover of responsibility to ensure continuity in local governance.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the bank statements.

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Budget Officer in regard to the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure reliability of financial information and proper monitoring of available disaster funds.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Review/ analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and

Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized government depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson, together with the other members of the LDRRM Council, comply with the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage to adequately prepare the Municipality when hazards/ disasters strike.

We further recommended that the LDRRMF be fully utilized for the implementation of projects and activities contained in the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the year to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials to:

Set-up and maintain a GAD Database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;

Ensure timely preparation and submission of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report in required format together with necessary documents to the DILG Regional Office VI for review and approval to ascertain compliance with the guidelines; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, spearhead the efficient and effective enforcement of the Municipal Environment Ordinance in compliance with RA No. 9003 by directing the concerned officials to:

Require and/or observe strict implementation of mandatory segregation of solid wastes from source to disposal facility, and impose appropriate fines/ sanctions for violators;

Continuously intensify information, education and communication campaign on proper waste segregation and disposal, and the necessity of the establishment of a Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) to the community through coordination with the officials of the barangays;
Take appropriate actions to provide adequate facilities to the MRF so that it will be fully operational; and
Collect garbage fees from both residential and commercial areas for Solid Waste Management services rendered, as provided in the Ordinance.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the moneys withheld for the BIR and GSIS, and remit intact the full amounts to the concerned government agencies within the required period pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005 and RA No. 8291, otherwise the interests, penalties and surcharges on late remittances will be the personal liability of the concerned Municipal officials.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

The Municipal Accountant to verify and reconcile the differences/ variances in the Due to NGAs, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if any, to conform with the amounts provided by the DILG, and to present the correct amount of the liabilities in the financial statements;

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1 and 24, by submitting the complete set of financial statements, to include the SCBAA and supporting disclosure of an explanation of the reasons for material differences, if any, to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the SEF in accordance to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

The Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to monitor the receipts and corresponding disbursements of funds, ensure the completeness of the liquidation reports and supporting documents, in accordance with Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and submit the same to the IPG and IA Audit Team, if such projects are already implemented;

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LSB prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality to disburse funds in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 5447. The utilization of the SEF shall focus in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Municipal officials to give priority for the implementation of allowable projects/activities included in the school board budget for the year.

We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized by the Municipality according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall take the lead in mainstreaming GAD in LGU PPAs. The GFPS shall adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal officials strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and DBM BC No. 2014-03. They shall see to it that payments of incentives to government personnel be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing payments of such to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.    95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO-Designate to see to it that all purchases of gasoline, fuel and oil are supported with complete documentation, all DTTs are properly accomplished and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 77-61.
We recommended that the LGU officials and employees strictly adhere to the provisions of NBC No. 486, as amended.

We also recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials and employees to refund their excess claims considering the requirement that any amount in excess of the prescribed rate shall be at the expense of the participants.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 by submitting a copy of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its execution.
We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the CY 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,341,420.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the RPT Registers or Individual Taxpayer’s Index Cards duly certified by the Treasurer as basis in setting-up SET Receivables at the beginning of the year to ensure accuracy and to avoid misstatement of the SET Budget.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and her staff to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts and to coordinate with concerned officials to obtain information on long outstanding accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile with the Province. Due to the eruption of Taal volcano on January 12, 2020 the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were not able to reconcile and confirm the accounts in the Province. A request by the Province for confirmation of balances is yet to be acted upon by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The reconciliation with the Province has not been undertaken due to disruptions from the Taal volcano eruption; the Provincial request for confirmation is yet to be acted on by the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended LGU consider guidelines on Local Economic Enterprises, strictly enforce Market Code provisions, ensure full collection of market rights, execute lease contracts, maintain daily records and receipts, adopt marketing strategies and improve management.
observations.managementAction: Municipality acquired satellite market stalls records; Office of Economic Enterprise to exert effective marketing strategies. Municipal Treasurer requested BPLO to implement improvements. However, full collection and proper contracts are still lacking.
observations.reason: Management did not prioritize corrective action; lack of executed contracts and full collection processes remain; no consistent advertising and promotion done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile balances with the Province and furnish copies of JEVs showing collections by the Municipality to the Provincial Accountant.
observations.managementAction: No reconciliation was completed with the Province owing to disruptions from the Taal volcano eruption; the Provincial request for confirmation remains pending and the Municipal Accountant plans to act upon receiving the request.
observations.reason: Reconciliation delayed due to Taal volcano eruption; pending Provincial confirmation and Municipal action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant refrain from taking up interest income as Due to Other Funds in Trust Fund books and effect transfer of the amount to General Fund and record properly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared to effect the transfer of ₱51,254.49 to General Fund and will draw the necessary entries.
observations.reason: The transfer of interest income was not yet effected as of audit date; Municipal Accountant to effect the transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all ongoing and planned government projects/programs/activities in compliance with Section 2.0 and 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that due to the sudden eruption of Taal volcano they were not able to prepare and submit the required reports but assured compliance in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: No submission at beginning of year; disruption due to Taal volcano eruption cited as reason for non-submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to retrieve required documents and facilitate titling; the Municipal Legal Officer to execute Deed of Donation where applicable and secure supporting documents required for land purchases.
observations.managementAction: The GSO scheduled inspections and acquisition of documents; the Municipal Legal Officer resigned and there were delays. The Municipal Accountant recorded unrecorded parcels of land via JEV No. 100-2019-07-106 dated 7/15/2019.
observations.reason: Deed of Donation and complete titling documentation were not fully processed; staff changes and missing documents caused delays.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Sangguniang Bayan and Local Chief Executive terminate/rescind the Deeds of Assignment, strictly observe guidelines on lease of government property, impose past due penalties/damages to PAPISSS, Inc. and submit updated list of legitimate stallholders and monitor occupancy rate regularly.
observations.managementAction: Management is yet to convene department heads and stakeholders to address the issues; the Municipal Treasurer reported timely payments by Philippine Primark Properties for 2018-2019 and Bagsakan Area rents, but no termination/rescission was made. No penalties were imposed.
observations.reason: Management did not act to rescind DOAs; actions pending convening of stakeholders and further legal/administrative study.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the following: The Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties; and likewise to require department/office heads to submit lists of properties existing in their respective offices to the Committee for validation and consolidation;
The Inventory Committee to prepare the inventory report and submit to the Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO for reconciliation, and to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
The Designated GSO and Municipal Accountant to prepare, maintain and update the property cards and PPE ledger cards for easy monitoring, proper identification and validation of properties;
The Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO to reconcile their records and make appropriate adjustments, if any, to present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements;

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to: Review and update the LDRRMFIP  annually to include list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years;

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Budget Officer in regard to the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure reliability of financial information and proper monitoring of available disaster funds.
We also recommended that the LCE provide additional personnel qualified to occupy the three (3) staff positions under the LDRRMO.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1 and 24, by submitting the complete set of financial statements, to include the SCBAA and supporting disclosure of an explanation of the reasons for material differences, if any, to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads/Division Chiefs to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2017 AAR recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/divisions, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted to the COA Auditor within sixty (60) days from the receipt of the AAR, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.
We also recommended that in case of settlement of disallowances on installment basis, the persons liable shall request from the COA for approval of the monthly installment payments.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has no sufficient fund to establish a sanitary landfill but alternative means for the protection of public health and the environment was undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the following:
• The Inventory Committee conduct regular reconciliation of RPCPPE with the
accounting records to fully identify the discrepancies noted. Considering
these findings on PPE as a perennial issue of the Municipality, we recommend
that these procedures be done by account until all PPE accounts in the
financial statements are completed/reconciled;
• The Accounting Department in coordination with the GSO exhaust all efforts to
fully identify the PPEs included in the lump-sum/unidentified balances. Secure
the necessary documents to support those PPEs to be able to reconcile with
the Inventory Report of the GSO. Otherwise, provide disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements.
• The Inventory Committee locate and secure missing/unaccounted properties.
If whereabouts cannot be determined after exerting all efforts, trace
accountability and require the persons accountable to prepar the incident
report indicating the reasons of missing properties and if found negligent he shall pay the missing properties at cost;
• The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all properties of the
municipality, preparation of the corresponding report and reconciliation of
inventory results against the accounting records.
• The Inventory Committee report the result of the inventory count of Road
Networks in the format prescribed under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting
Office the List of Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of the books every
year for the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts, in order to
fairly present the said accounts in the financial statements; and
b) the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the payees for the existence of
stale checks and require submission of requirements to facilitate replacement of
new checks; and
c) the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of Unreleased
Checks treated as reconciling items/outstanding checks at the end of the
year/closing of books, as well as the reversion of stale checks for an accurate
and reliable accounts of Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable balances in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to present an accurate and
reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust
all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and
encourage the taxpayer to pay their dues on time. Conduct verification on those
outstanding balances to determine the accuracy of the receivables and its collectibility.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her
staff to:
• continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their
records on receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables
in the books of the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-
agency accounts.
• coordinate with the concerned officials and departments heads to obtain
information on those long outstanding accounts. Secure the necessary
documents to properly identify the creditor/local government unit to whom it’s
due. Once the specific local government unit had been identified, verify the
reported balances in the subsidiary ledgers through confirmation letters and
exhaust efforts to fully ascertain the validity and legality of those obligations.
As to the financial assistance from the Province secure copies of agreement to
determine provisions on the unexpended balance. Otherwise, coordinate with
the Province for proper guidance on those long outstanding balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
recognize in the books of accounts the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting
up of Accounts Payable (AP) for goods received or services rendered, inspected and
accepted, and for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and
inspected, to fairly present the Due to BIR account at yearend and timely remit those
taxes to the BIR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the system provider, to update the permit fees embedded in the
system on financial institutions, poultry & livestock farming (agri-business) and on
newly established business so as to be paralleled with the 2017 Revised Revenue Code
of Lemery which will consequently result to proper assessment of fees.

Likewise, we recommended the BPLO to ensure that validation of the essential
information as basis of assessment and collection of fees be undertaken to come up
with an accurate assessed permit fee to avoid under collection of income and ensure
that maintenance of these records be accordingly prepared.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Officer to include the utilization of the unexpended balance
for DRRM, which is under the Trust Liability – DRRM, in the LDRRMFIP for
programs/projects/activities related to disaster management and prevention.

Likewise, ensure the optimal utilization and implementation of the programs and
projects funded out of Disaster Mitigation Fund and Continuing Appropriation within
the planned timeline.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all on-
going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented during the year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.

Since the Municipality opted to attribute a portion of its budget to the GAD Budget,
we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point System
to constantly attend a training program on how to administer the Harmonized Gender
and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in its GAD Plan and Budget to assure the proper
determination of the PPAs that may be attributed to the GAD Budget.

Likewise, we recommended that HGDG test be administered again at the end of
project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of
PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.

We reiterated the following recommendations cited in the 2017 Annual Audit Report:
a) that the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive terminate/rescind
the Deed of Assignment entered into by PAPISSS, Inc., Prestonburg
Development Corporation and Philippine Primark Properties, Inc. and strictly
observe the proper guidelines on lease of government property in order to
protect the interest of the Municipality and its constituents.
b) that the Municipality impose the corresponding past due penalties and damages
to PAPISSS, Inc. for the non-performance of its obligations.
Lastly, we recommended the Municipality to immediately submit an updated list of
legitimate stallholders and ensure that monitoring of the occupancy rate of the
Public Market be consistently undertaken in order to determine the appropriate lease
payment due to the Municipality and rightfully enforce its right thereto.
