   a. The Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of  all
      properties and equipment of the Municipality and to prepare and submit
      the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor,  pursuant  to  the
      pertinent provisions of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs;


   b. The Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain and update  the
      PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for proper identification of  all  PPEs;
      and

   c. The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with  the
      submitted RPCPPE  in  order  to  present  accurate  and  reliable  PPE
      balances in the financial statements.

Recommendation:


   We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to coordinate with the Acting Municipal  Treasurer  to  ensure
   that all unreleased checks be reported and a  Journal  Entry  Voucher  be
   prepared by  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  record  the  entry  for  the
   restoration of cash equivalent to the unreleased checks  and  recognition
   of the appropriate liability accounts.

Recommendations:


   We recommended the following:


   a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE)  to  direct  the  concerned  Municipal
      officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund  immediately  their
      unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 339  of  RA
      No. 7160 and Section 89 of PD  No.  1445,  to  avoid  accumulation  of
      unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair  presentation  of  the
      relevant accounts in the financial statements;


   b. The Municipal Accountant to adhere to pertinent provisions and  ensure
      that no cash advances shall be allowed to  any  official  or  employee
      unless the previous cash  advance  granted  is  settled  or  a  proper
      accounting thereof is made;


   c. The LCE to direct the imposition  of  appropriate  sanctions  for  the
      unjustified failure of the concerned Municipal officials and employees
      to liquidate their cash advances; and


   d. The LCE to direct the concerned  Municipal  officials  to  follow  the
      guidelines and procedures set  in  COA  Circular  No.  2016-005  dated
      December 19, 2016 on the write-off, if warranted, of the  unliquidated
      cash advances which have been long outstanding/remained dormant in the
      books of accounts.

Recommendations:


   We recommended the following:


   a. The Local Chief Executive  (LCE),  as  Chairman,  and  all  the  other
      members of the Municipal Development Council  (MDC),  to  prepare  the
      Annual Investment Plan containing priority development projects to  be
      funded out of the 20% DF, to include only  specific  and  identifiable
      PPAs with corresponding quantifiable targets,  attainable  within  the
      desired time frame, and consistent with the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC
      No. 2017-1;  Moreover, the  MDC  and  other  heads  of  offices  shall
      regularly  monitor  the  progress  of  the  projects,  their   timely,
      effective and efficient implementation, and the  commitment  from  the
      concerned offices to implement the PPAs as planned; and


   b. The LCE to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally and/or  fully  utilized
      in accordance with the guidelines  to  achieve  the  desirable  socio-
      economic development  and  environmental  outcomes  and  maximize  the
      benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the  concerned
   municipal officials to:


   a. Observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.  2013-1  in
      formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF and to ensure the full
      utilization of the Fund according to the  guidelines  to  achieve  the
      maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents; and


   b. Review and revise/update the LDRRMFIP regularly to include the list of
      specific projects and activities to be charged against the  unexpended
      LDRRMF of the previous years, subject to the  approval  of  the  local
      sanggunian and the LCE.

Recommendation:


   We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
   municipal officials and employees to ensure the full utilization  of  the
   allocated budget for  plans/programs  that  will  address  gender  issues
   identified for  the  realization  of  GAD  objectives  which  are  Gender
   Equality and Women Empowerment.

Recommendations:


   We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE)  and  concerned
   municipal officials to  refrain  from  conducting  seminar,  training  or
   similar activity out of town  and  to  give  preference  to  the  use  of
   government-owned venues.


   We also recommended that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee  to
   strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA  No.  9184  by
   ensuring that all procurement  shall  be  done  in  accordance  with  the
   prescribed guidelines so that most  advantageous  terms,  conditions  and
   prices are obtained by the government.


   Furthermore, we recommended that the LCE require the concerned  municipal
   officials and employees to submit the lacking  documents  and  to  ensure
   that all claims against government  funds  are  supported  with  complete
   documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

Recommendation:


   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  MENRO-Designate
   and other members of the Solid Waste  Management  Board  to  ensure  that
   proper revisions be  made  to  the  Municipality’s  10-year  Solid  Waste
   Management Plan  and  have  it  approved  by  the  National  Solid  Waste
   Management Commission.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enforce COE Nos. 2016-
   083 and 2016-084, by requiring  the  withholding  of  salaries  or  other
   compensation  due  the  persons  liable  in  satisfaction  of  the  audit
   disallowances.


   We also recommended that  in  case  disallowances  shall  be  settled  by
   installment payments,  the  persons  liable  request  from  the  COA  for
   approval to allow them to pay such on installment basis.


   We further recommended that the LCE direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to
   coordinate with the Acting Municipal Treasurer  in  the  preparation  and
   submission of an updated  schedule  of  unsettled  disallowances  of  all
   persons liable with details of the settlements and  supporting  documents
   for issuance of NSSDC/s and reconciliation of accounting records with the
   issued SASDC.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to:


   a. Make necessary adjusting entries for the improper recognition  of  the
      fund transfers, and properly record subsequent releases in  the  books
      of the Municipality to  reflect  correct  balances  in  the  financial
      statements;


   b.  Ensure  that  all  required  documents  such  as  the  Memorandum  of
      Agreement, Approved Estimated Expenses,  Official  Receipt,  etc.  are
      accomplished and attached to the Disbursement Vouchers  prior  to  the
      release of funds;


   c. Monitor the utilization of the  funds  released  to  the  implementing
      agencies by requiring submission of liquidation reports; and


   d. Require refund of the unexpended balance of the fund transfer, if any,
      evidenced by an Official Receipt.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct all  concerned
   municipal  officials  to  prioritize  the   conduct   of   the   physical
   inventory/count of properties to ascertain their existence and condition.


   We also recommended that the  LCE  coordinate  with  the  Municipal  Vice
   Mayor, who is the  former  Municipal  Mayor,  to  comply  with  the  DILG
   Memorandum  Circular  No.  2019-39   for   the   official   turnover   of
   responsibility to ensure continuity in local governance.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  all  the  concerned
   Department Heads/Divisions Chiefs to submit their action plans and status
   of implementation on the CY 2018 AAR recommendations  including  that  of
   prior years, related/connected to  their  department/divisions,  and  the
   information gathered therein be used to properly  accomplish  the  AAPSI,
   and submit the same to the COA Auditor.


   We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI  be  submitted  to
   the Auditor within sixty (60) days from the receipt of the AAR, either in
   printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) the General Service Officer to –
o conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment; o accomplish the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the prescribed format; o submit the RPCPPE to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; b) the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to reconcile the differences in order not to compromise the reliability of its submitted reports; c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Card for each category of assets and reconcile with the balance presented in the financial statements; d) the Municipal Accountant to fully identify the PPE with lump sum balances and secure the necessary documents to reconcile it with the Inventory Report of GSO; e) the General Services Officer to have an updated list of unserviceable properties for disposal and prepare all the necessary documents/reports to act on the unserviceable properties for disposal within the reasonable time to prevent further deterioration of the PPE and the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosure in the notes to financial statements in relation to the reported unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The RPCPPE submitted by the management pertains to Property Plant and Equipment under the General Fund. No physical count was conducted on properties recorded in the Special Education Fund. The GSO reconciled its inventory report with the Accounting records in order to update the records. The Municipal Assessor and the General Services Office coordinated on the titling of land parcels. The Municipal Accountant booked up unrecorded parcels of land in 2019. The Municipal Accountant prepared journal entry JEV No. 100-2019-07-106 dated 7/15/2019 to record unrecorded parcels of land.
observations.reason: Other properties still posed which are not disclosed in the report which the GSO is still working on with the Accounting Office. No physical count was conducted on properties under the Special Education Fund. The GSO is in the process of completing the necessary documents for disposal. The Municipal Assessor and GSO are scheduling inspections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned offices to effect proper adjustments on the cancelled check and stale checks and that the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipal books of accounts to fairly present its financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The cancellation of the check was recorded under JEV No. 100-2019-02-019 dated 02/28/2019 as well as the cancelled check under JEV No. 100-2019-08-124 dated 8/13/2019 and JEV No. 200-2019-08-033 dated 8/15/2019. The Municipal Accountant implemented the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPT/SET receivables and that the Municipal Mayor, through the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and adopt strict policies and more effective measures of collection to minimize RPT delinquencies and report certified lists of delinquencies to the Sanggunian Bayan for assistance in enforcement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with the name and the amount due and collectible for the year instead of list of barangays with the total tax due of all taxpayers. The Municipal Accountant reconciled the noted differences and started creating subsidiary ledgers for these accounts. The Municipal Treasurer is enhancing collection measures and submitting certified lists to the Sangguniang Bayan and the Provincial Treasurer for civil remedies.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was provided with the certified list and reconciled differences, but further work is ongoing to fully reconcile and create subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to PPSAS by classifying the current portion of long-term loans payable that is due for settlement within a year as part of current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Payable is now separately presented from its non-current portion as of year-end. A journal entry was drawn to correctly classify the current portion.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the current portion; the current portion of loans payable is now presented separately as part of current liabilities as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: properly ensure cash advances are liquidated as soon as the purpose was served or within 30 days after return; immediately issue demand letters enjoining accountable officials to liquidate outstanding cash advances; enforce a cut off in disbursing cash advances and withhold salary of accountable officers who fail to settle despite demand letters; and exercise diligence in monitoring liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Of the total amount, ₱9,261.00 was refunded on May 10, 2019 and ₱30,000.00 was settled on July 12, 2019. The Municipal Accountant implemented cut-off measures in 2nd semester of CY2020. Delays were still noted in liquidation of wages/allowance/honoraria despite follow-ups.
observations.reason: There were still delays in liquidation of some cash advances despite repeated follow ups; implementation of stricter cut-offs was instituted in the 2nd semester of 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of cash equivalent to the interest income from the Trust Fund to the General Fund so that the amount could be made available for appropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant indicated that the bank charges in other Trust Fund accounts would be deducted and that the amount could still be transferred after such deductions. The amount will be made available for appropriation.
observations.reason: Transfer has not yet been completed as bank charges applicable to other Trust Fund accounts are to be considered; action pending deduction of charges.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), being the focal unit, to strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-002 in preparing the LDRRMFIP and coordinate with MDRRM Office in enforcing implementation of projects; and ensure efficient utilization of the 20% Local Development Fund to deliver desirable social, economic and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO ensured optimal utilization and 27 of 30 projects listed in the 2019 AIP were accomplished; 2 were bidded and 1 was 11% complete as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDO to observe COA Circular 2012-002 in preparing the LDRRMFIP, include projects charged to unexpended balances, coordinate with the MDRRM Officer, and ensure the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF is submitted and amounts reconciled and disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal MPDO coordinated with the MDRRMO for implementation; the Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 300-2019-01-003 to transfer unexpended balance. The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was submitted to the Office of the Auditor and is being reconciled.
observations.reason: Some amounts were reverted and reported, but full reconciliation and disclosure are ongoing; certain balances remain to be reconciled and disclosed per COA format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Executive Committee and Technical Working Group adhere to the complete GFPS structure and positions; ensure participation of all members in GAD activities; formulate gender issues from the GAD database; forward database to PPDO and DILG; conduct early planning and budgeting for GAD and ensure compliance with JMC timeline; and assess compliance using the GAD checklist.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC provided PPDO their formulated GAD Program; trainings and seminars on GAD were conducted in Aug and Nov 2019 with participation. The GAD Focal Person committed to adopt HGDG tool and conduct PIMME at end of implementation.
observations.reason: GAD institutionalization and systematic application of HGDG still need further training and full implementation; CY2019 GPB submission timing was not in full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended HRMO require Department Heads to include specific tasks, number of man hours, duration, location and quantify work in job order requests; coordinate to assess organizational structure and ideal plantilla to minimize job order hiring and include positions in recruitment.
observations.managementAction: HRMO and Department Heads now include requested information in MPDC requests. Creation of additional plantilla positions was requested to Sanggunian Bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan. Accomplishment reports became more detailed and quantified.
observations.reason: Budgetary limitations and ongoing approval of additional positions; full reorganization pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended MPDC coordinate procurement considering regular activities, BAC ensure completeness and validity of bidding documents, and impose liquidated damages as required under IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is now carefully checking bidding documents and evaluating eligibility. The contractor provided missing documents; liquidation of damages was not imposed as the contractor claimed delays were due to oversight on Notice of Suspension dates.
observations.reason: Liquidated damages were not imposed due to contractor's claim that delays were due to issuance timing of Notice of Suspension; further action pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended MPDO adhere to JMC 2018-61 and consider activities that may affect implementation during planning; MEO to put on record project acceptance dates and ensure warranty security issuance; BAC to invite observers, notify losing bidders, and follow procurement timelines.
observations.managementAction: MPDO now consolidates and plans activities to avoid suspensions. MEO maintains project records and photos; BAC invites observers and follows posting requirements; Municipal Accountant checks supporting documents before payment.
observations.reason: Some procurement and documentation improvements made, but dated photos and full documentation compliance still had gaps; some notices and postings now handled properly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended BAC ensure completeness of bidding documents and eligibility checks, verify PCAB license and contractor legitimacy before awarding contracts.
observations.managementAction: BAC now checks and evaluates eligibility documents to ensure propriety; the required documents were submitted and validated. The Approved Budget and Contract documents were furnished to the Office.
observations.reason: BAC improved procedures but initial lapses occurred; full compliance achieved subsequently.

observations.recommendation: We recommended BAC consolidate purchase requests, adhere to PPMP requirements and timelines, and ensure procurement planning to avoid piecemeal purchases.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat was instructed to require submission of consolidated PPMP 45 days prior to project implementation; MPDC added strict implementation for second semester 2019 and commenced further actions in 2020.
observations.reason: Some purchases continued on piecemeal basis prior to full implementation of consolidation measures; stronger controls instituted thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local School Board formulate PPAs for ECCD charged to SEF, and the Municipal Accountant evaluate entries and prepare adjusting entries to reclassify items recorded in the wrong accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer commented on budget preparation and the Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 200-2019-08-032 dated 8/14/2019 to reclassify items to accurately present financial statements.
observations.reason: Some ECCD PPAs were prepared but procurement and classification issues required journal adjustments which were made; ongoing refinements continue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Engineer correct deficiencies noted by TAS through inspection and request another inspection to validate completeness, otherwise demand payment from contractor including liquidated damages for unfinished work.
observations.managementAction: The contractor corrected some deficiencies. Inspection was conducted by the MEO and partial compliance was satisfied. A follow-up inspection was requested to validate completeness.
observations.reason: Correction of deficiencies was only partially complied with by the contractor; further inspection requested but pending full validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require HRMO to discontinue approving loan amortizations that reduce monthly net take home pay below ₱5,000 and to validate payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management sent letter to former Administrator requesting nullification of salary increase and adjustment; former Administrator was requested to refund ₱4,396.00 which was received on April 2, 2018. The Municipal management discontinued some deductions.

observations.recommendation: We again recommended that: a) the General Service Officer maintain updated inventory report and RPCPPE; b) GSO recognize receipts and dispositions of property; c) Municipal Accountant maintain updated subsidiary ledgers and reconcile with GSO; d) the Municipal Mayor require immediate disposal action on unserviceable properties and provide Notes disclosures.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted inventory reports and the Municipal Accountant booked up unrecorded assets. Public auction for unserviceable properties was held but was not successful as there were no bidders. The Municipal Accountant recorded unrecorded parcels of land in 2019.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE was submitted for General Fund only; Special Education Fund properties still pending physical count. Public auction attempt was unsuccessful and further actions are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for current and prior years to provide detailed account records and to reconcile differences per NGAS requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant maintains records of Real Property Tax payments starting CY2016 and has been reconciling. Creation of subsidiary ledgers started but full historical ledger work remains extensive.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers for prior years are not fully maintained due to workload and need for additional staff; reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and request Sanggunian authority for reversion to Unappropriated Surplus as per RA No. 3526 and EO No. 109 s.1993.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant evaluated and prepared a request for reversion of long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus with supporting documents; partial reversion of certain amounts was approved.
observations.reason: Only portion of outstanding payables was reverted; further evaluation and approval processes remain for remaining items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit collections due to national agencies to Bureau of Treasury and request authority from Sanggunian to revert long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus.
observations.managementAction: Portion of unexpended balance from BUB totaling ₱128,380.44 was remitted to Bureau of Treasury under JEV No. 300-2018-04-017 dated April 4, 2018. Other remittances are being coordinated.
observations.reason: Only certain amounts remitted; some trust funds remain to be remitted or reverted pending coordination.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to set up and maintain special accounts and subsidiary ledgers for the 20% Development Fund and economic enterprises to serve as decision-making tools and monitor performance.
observations.managementAction: For CY2019 the Municipal Accountant recorded some related transactions but still failed to set up separate special accounts in the General Fund for all economic enterprises; some subsidiary ledgers were prepared.
observations.reason: Not all special accounts and subsidiary ledgers were set up; more work required to fully implement accounting segregation for 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor ensure efficient and effective utilization of 20% Development Fund, itemize projects in Annual Budget, and adopt project monitoring measures.
observations.managementAction: Of the 30 projects listed, 27 were completed, 2 were bidded and 1 was 11% complete as of year-end. Annual Budget includes items and Municipal Accountant recorded project expenditures appropriately.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEVs to transfer unexpended balances to Trust Liability, include unexpended balances in LDRRMFIP, and ensure Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF is submitted and reconciled.
observations.managementAction: Reversion of CY2011-2013 unexpended balance was effected under SB Resolution No. 19-2929 dated May 10, 2019. The Municipal Accountant effected JEVs and disclosed in the notes to financial statements the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Auction and Satellite Market Office to justify the necessity of the satellite market operation, adopt strategies to make the market viable, encourage stallholders/investors to lease, and conduct study on utilization of purchased land for income generation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality planned strategies to encourage possible investors and companies to lease and utilize the area; some studies were initiated. However, actions were limited and management had not convened stakeholders to finalize solutions.
observations.reason: Management has not fully implemented market improvements and has not convened stakeholders; study and investor solicitation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to initiate processing of GSIS insurance policies for various properties, furnish GSIS the inventory report for appraisal and premium computation, and ensure compliance with R.A. No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office initiated the conduct of inventory and submitted list of insurable properties to the GSIS; insurance premium for 2019 amounted to ₱255,827.81. The GSO is coordinating with GSIS for appraisal and premium computations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO conduct site inspection and prepare reports on actual usage, the Municipal Assessor retrieve necessary information and refer titling matters to concerned agencies, the Municipal Legal Officer execute Deed of Donation where applicable, and the Municipal Accountant recognize assessed values of unrecorded parcels in LGU books.
observations.managementAction: GSO scheduled inspections; Municipal Accountant booked unrecorded parcels with JEV No. 100-2019-07-106 dated 7/15/2019. Deed of Donation for certain parcels was reported missing and under further evaluation.
observations.reason: Title transfers and complete documentation remain pending; Deed of Donation could not be located and legal processes remain to be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert diligent effort to locate receivables supporting documents; if locate efforts fail, request write-off except for disallowances/charges in accordance with COA rules.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were undertaken to locate documents and employees responsible; the Municipal Accountant continues to pursue collection and validation. For receivables deemed valid, collection through salary deductions is being pursued; write-off requests to COA to be made if necessary.
observations.reason: Some receivables were validated and collection pursued; others lacked supporting documents and remain subject to further validation or write-off request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require HRMO to nullify improper salary increases, require refund of overpayments where applicable, and ensure CSC rules are complied with.
observations.managementAction: Municipal management sent letter requesting former Administrator to nullify the salary increase and refund amount of ₱4,396.00; partial refunds were received. HRMO adjusted practices and ensured compliance.
observations.reason: Refund of ₱4,396.00 was processed; systemic compliance to CSC rules being reinforced but prior irregularities needed correction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Engineer coordinate with the contractor for refund of the excessive amount and implement appropriate corrective measures.
observations.managementAction: An Appeal Memorandum dated April 2, 2018 was filed by the LGU and received by COA Regional Office IV-A on May 18, 2018 regarding ND No. 17-001 (12-14). Subsequent adjustments and recoveries are being processed.
observations.reason: The LGU filed an appeal and adjustments are under review by COA; recovery actions underway but not fully resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the bank statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and/or OIC-Municipal Treasurer secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the bank statements after the end of each month for the timely preparation of the BRS.
Furthermore, we recommended that the LCE provide additional accounting staff to assist the Municipal Accountant to prepare the various financial reports.
The Designated GSO to issue ARE to Municipal officials and employees concerned to cover the equipment issued for proper control and accountability of equipment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to make the following adjustments in the Financial Statements: Transfer the  unexpended/ unobligated balance of the DRRMF-MOOE and QRF under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books;

Revert the unutilized LDRRMF for CYs 2010-2013 amounting to ₱4,943,401.56 to the unappropriated surplus of the GF and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended for the imposition of appropriate sanctions for the unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances.

We further recommended that the Municipality shall follow the guidelines and procedures set in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the write-off, if warranted, of the unliquidated cash advances which have been long outstanding/ remained dormant in the books.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to: Review/ analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts;

Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized government depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended the following: The LCE, as Chairman, and all the other members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to prepare the AIP containing priority development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include only specific and identifiable Programs/ Projects/Activities (PPAs) with corresponding quantifiable targets, attainable within the desired time frame, and consistent with the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1;  Moreover, the MPDC and other heads of offices shall regularly monitor the progress of the projects, their timely, effective and efficient implementation, and the commitment from the concerned offices to implement the PPAs as planned;

Ensure that the 20% DF is optimally and/or fully utilized in accordance with the guidelines to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and maximize the benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson, together with the other members of the LDRRM Council, comply with the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage to adequately prepare the Municipality when hazards/ disasters strike.

We further recommended that the LDRRMF be fully utilized for the implementation of projects and activities contained in the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the year to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.
We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials to: Set-up and maintain a GAD Database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;

Ensure timely preparation and submission of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report in required format together with necessary documents to the DILG Regional Office VI for review and approval to ascertain compliance with the guidelines; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, spearhead the efficient and effective enforcement of the Municipal Environment Ordinance in compliance with RA No. 9003 by directing the concerned officials to: Require and/or observe strict implementation of mandatory segregation of solid wastes from source to disposal facility, and impose appropriate fines/ sanctions for violators;

Continuously intensify information, education and communication campaign on proper waste segregation and disposal, and the necessity of the establishment of a Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) to the community through coordination with the officials of the barangays;
Take appropriate actions to provide adequate facilities to the MRF so that it will be fully operational;
Collect garbage fees from both residential and commercial areas for Solid Waste Management services rendered, as provided in the Ordinance.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the moneys withheld for the BIR and GSIS, and remit intact the full amounts to the concerned government agencies within the required period pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005 and RA No. 8291, otherwise the interests, penalties and surcharges on late remittances will be the personal liability of the concerned Municipal officials.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following: The Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to monitor the receipts and corresponding disbursements of fund transfers, ensure the completeness of the liquidation reports and supporting documents in accordance with Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and submit the same to the DILG RO VI and the Audit Team, if such projects were already implemented;

The Municipal Accountant to verify and reconcile the differences/ variances in the Due to NGAs, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if any, to conform with the amounts provided by the DILG, and to present the correct amount of the liabilities in the financial statements;

The Municipal Engineer to undertake the procurement process if projects had not been implemented, and to observe the agreed prescribed period, with strict compliance to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, as well as budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations;
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to refund/return to the IPG the unexpended balance of the fund transfer upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the SEF in accordance to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization for monitoring, transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the LCE enforce the COE Nos. 2016-083 and 2016-084, by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the audit disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following: The Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to monitor the receipts and corresponding disbursements of funds, ensure the completeness of the liquidation reports and supporting documents, in accordance with Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and submit the same to the IPG and IA Audit Team, if such projects are already implemented;

The Municipal Engineer to undertake the procurement process for the unimplemented projects,  and implement these within the prescribed period agreed in the MOA, with strict compliance to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, as well as budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations;
The OIC - Municipal Treasurer to refund/return to the IPG the unexpended balance of the fund transfer upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LSB prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality to disburse funds in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 5447. The utilization of the SEF shall focus in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Municipal officials to give priority for the implementation of allowable projects/activities included in the school board budget for the year.

We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized by the Municipality according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect transfer of interest income from the Trust Fund books to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made several attempts to request fund transfer but were refused by the MTO due to maintaining balance concerns. During the exit conference, management concurred and agreed the amount pertaining to interest earned from Trust Fund bank accounts will be transferred to the General Fund net of the ₱10,000.00 maintaining balance.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer refused issuance of check for transfer citing maintaining balance concerns and potential bank charges. As of report date, transfer had not been effected; the amount remained in the Trust Fund. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 7, page 54)

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor ensure compliance with CSC rules and require HRMO to nullify improper salary increases and require the former Municipal Administrator to refund unauthorized salary increase amounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management indicated they will nullify the Notice of Salary Increase given to the former Municipal Administrator and request refund of the amount of ₱4,396.00 from the former official.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Administrator received the amount and the Municipal management sent a letter requesting refund; as of report date, refund had not been effected and the former administrator had resigned; full recovery/action pending.

observations.recommendation: Consolidate all validated inventory reports from the respective offices, with the corresponding  property numbers for proper identification of all PPE, which shall be used as basis for the preparation of the PPE ledger cards and Inventory Report to be submitted by the Inventory Committee to the Auditor, Municipal Accountant and GSO;
The Accounting and General Services Offices to reconcile their records, and any discrepancies be adjusted to properly present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements; and
Prepare an updated ARE, with corresponding serial numbers, to be issued to concerned accountable officers/property holders.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the bank statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the bank statements after the end of each month for the timely preparation of the BRS.
We also recommended that in case of settlement of disallowances on installment basis, the persons liable shall request from the COA for approval of the monthly installment payments.

We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads/ Division Chiefs to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2016 AAR recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/divisions and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted to the COA Auditor within sixty (60) days from the receipt of the AAR, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Utilization of DRRMF to the Auditor within the reglementary period using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Monthly reports for the Utilization of DRRMF were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no sufficient budget/ fund to establish a sanitary landfill but alternative means for the protection of public health and the environment were undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the Real Property Tax Registers or Individual Taxpayer’s Index Cards duly certified by the Treasurer as basis in setting-up SET Receivables at the beginning of the year to ensure accuracy and to avoid misstatement of the SET Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a) the General Service Officer to –
conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment
accomplish the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) in the prescribed NGAS format
submit the RPCPPE to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of
each year;

b) the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to reconcile the
differences noted between the Inventory Report and Accounting records in
order not to compromise the reliability of submitted reports;

c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPE Ledger Card and Real
Property Ledger Card for each category of assets and reconcile with the
balance presented in the financial statements;

d) the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPE with
lump sum balances and at the same time secure the necessary documents to
reconcile it with the Inventory Report of GSO;

e) the Municipal General Services Officer to have an updated list of unserviceable
items for disposal and prepare all the necessary documents/reports to act on
the properties to be disposed within the reasonable time to prevent further
deterioration of the PPE and the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosure in
the notes to financial statements in relation to the reported unserviceable
properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale
checks and the appropriate adjustment on cancelled check and the Municipal
Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipal books of accounts to fairly
present its financial statements as of December 31, 2018. We also recommended that
the concerned offices establish proper coordination to avoid committing error that
may compromise the accuracy of its report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the aid of the Office of the
Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and adopt a strict and more effective policies and
measures of enforcing collection of Real Property Tax (RPT) in order to minimize the
report RPT Delinquencies each year. We also recommended that the Municipal
Treasurer submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a certified list of all real property tax
delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one year with a
request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continuously coordinate with the
Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to reconcile on a monthly basis or as
often as the transaction occurs all the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due
from/Due to LGUs and Loans Receivables/Payables by identifying the causes of
discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if warranted, in
order to show the correct balances of the inter-agency receivables and payables in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
adhere strictly to the provisions of the aforesaid PPSAS by classifying the current
portion of long-term loans payable that is due for settlement within a year from end of
reporting period as part of the current liabilities in the Statement of Financial
Position.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
• properly ensure that cash advances are liquidated as soon as the purpose
for which it was given has been served, or within 30 days after return to
permanent official station in case of local travel; if the purpose was not
served for the grant of cash advance, require the immediate refund of the
amount of cash advance;
• immediately issue demand letter enjoining the concerned official with
outstanding cash advance to liquidate the amount of ₱30,000.00;
• enforce a cut off in disbursing money out of the cash advance to timely
liquidate it and make a refund, if necessary.
• cause the withholding of salary of the Accountable Officers (AOs) who
failed to settle/liquidate their cash advances despite the issuance of
demand letters in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
and
• exercise utmost diligence in monitoring liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
effect the transfer of cash equivalent to the interest earned totaling ₱76,401.02 from
the Trust Fund to the General Fund so that the amount could be made available for
appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and/or the Municipal Engineer to optimally utilize the 20% Local
Development Fund in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and
environmental outcomes to the constituents which is essential in the attainment of
the constitutional objective.

In view of the observations above, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct:
a) the MPDO to observe the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 on preparing
the LDRRMFIP and also to coordinate with the MDRRM Office in enforcing
the implementation of the programs and projects within the planned
timeline for prompt delivery of the services for the benefit of the
constituents;
b) the LDRRM Officer ensure that the submitted Report on the Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF is compliant with the COA Circular 2012-002 and that the
amounts reflected in the report is accurate to avoid creating confusion to the
users of the report;
c) the Municipal Accountant prepare the journal entry for the transfer of the
unexpended LDRRMF balance of the 70% Mitigation Fund- MOOE and the
30% QRF as of year-end to proper Trust Liability account;
d) the Municipal Accountant provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial
Statements regarding the details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF and
e) revert to the unappropriated surplus the balance of the LDRRMF in the Trust
Liability which has exceeded the 5-year holding period to be made
available for the other social services.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender
equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Executive Committee
and the Technical Working Group:
• adhere to the complete structure and positions of the LGU GFPS provided for in
the MCW IRR and comply with the issuance of Memorandum for the official
designation of the GFPS TWG Chair;
• ensure the participation of all members of GFPS (Executive Committee and
Technical Working Group) in GAD sensitivity training (GST), gender-
responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis and gender audit in order
to sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and
development;
• see to it that the identified gender issues are formulated from the GAD
Database and forward the database to the province for consolidation.
Assistance of the DILG Operation Field Officer can be sought for as provided in
the Joint Memorandum Circular;
• conduct the early planning and budgeting for GAD so that the GPB can be
forwarded to the PPDO and DILG Regional Office for review and endorsement
within the scheduled timeframe; and
• assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the
elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on
those areas that should be in place.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) the HRMO in coordination with the requesting Department Head to incorporate
in the contract the specific task to be performed, number of man hours,
quantity and duration of the works to be performed by the job order personnel
and require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports; and
b) the HRMO in coordination with the Department Heads to assess its existing
organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be
established thereby minimizing, if not totally cannot be avoided, the hiring of job
order personnel.

Based on the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor instruct:
a) the MPDC in coordination with the BAC to carefully plan its procurement
process taking into consideration the regular activities of the agency occurring
throughout the year in order to avoid unnecessary deferment and delay in the
project implementation; and
b) the BAC to ensure the completeness, authenticity and validity of the bidding
documents and eligibility requirements submitted by the bidder.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials
to impose the liquidated damages on the incurred delay as prescribed by Section 8
Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
a) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), being the focal unit, to
strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2018-61 for the
implementation and monitoring of LGSF-AM Projects and to consider in the
planning stage all the activities that could affect the implementation of various
projects to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension not in accordance with IRR
of RA 9184.
b) Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to –
put on record the date when projects completed are accepted by the Municipality
to check if the warranty security were issued on proper dates.
to maintain construction records such that the pictures on the status of projects
completion must be dated.
to consider in the planning stage all the activities that could affect the
implementation of various infra projects to avoid the issuance of Notice of
Suspension not in accordance with IRR of RA 9184.
c) Bid and Awards Committee to strictly observe all the requirements of RA 9184 on
all their procurement such that:
observers were invited in all the stages of procurement as required by Section
13.1 of IRR of RA 9184.
the losing bidders will be furnished of the notifications of Bidding Results
indicated in Section 37.1.1 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
the recommended earliest possible time and maximum period allowed for the
procurement of infrastructure projects as shown in Annex C of IRR of RA 9184
will be followed with consideration on the quality of evaluation of bids
submitted and quality of post qualification conducted.
d) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly
supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001,
before making any payment, for proper control and to prevent improper or fraudulent
use of government funds and provide reasonable basis for establishing the validity of
claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to:
a. ensure completeness of the bidding documents to establish the propriety
of the bidding process. Submit all the lacking documents noted in this
report.
b. check, verify and validate first all the eligibility documents submitted by the
bidders before awarding contracts to bonafide contractors; and
c. observe the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the posting of Notice of
Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contract in the
agency website and PhilGEPS and require the BAC Secretariat to issue
certification on the posting of aforementioned documents in any
conspicuous places in the premises of the procuring entity.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat be required to consolidate the purchase
requests received from various offices/departments and procure the same in
accordance with the intent and requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal Officials
to exert intensive effort to adopt effective marketing strategies to encourage possible
stallholders or investors to rent or lease available space in the satellite market in
order to increase the occupancy rate therein.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality duly consider the instructive
guidelines on the implementing procedures for existing local economic enterprise
provided in the Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises
for Local Government Units 2016.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer strictly enforce the full
collection of the market right fees, execute a contract of lease agreement with the
stallholders of the satellite market synchronized with the market codes in order to
protect the rights of the Municipal Government of Lemery and impose corresponding
penalty for non-payment of arrears.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Office of the Municipal
Treasurer to maintain records to monitor the rental payments of the stallholders to
avoid possible loss of income from these stalls and strictly enforce the provisions of
the Market Code on imposing the rental rate and manner of payment and collection of
such.

Lastly, we recommended that the Office of the Economic Enterprise exert effort to
improve the occupancy rate in the satellite market and institute corrective actions on
the noted inconsistencies above.

We recommended that the Local School Board Council formulate additional
programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for health, nutrition, early education and social
services development related to the Early Childhood Care Development (ECCD)
Program that will directly address the basic holistic needs of young children and
promote their optimum growth and development.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to guide the Local School Board
Council in the formulation of the yearly PPAs charged against the Special Education
Fund specifically the PPAs in ECCD Program in line with the provisions of the afore-
cited law and regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
evaluate the entries made and prepare adjusting entries to reclassify items that had
been recorded in the Buildings account to fairly present its financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to impose
from the contractor the immediate correction of the deficiencies noted in the TAS
Inspection and Evaluation Report and request for another inspection upon
compliance thereon to validate completeness of the project as per plans and
specifications. Otherwise, demand payment from the contractor the amount of
₱262,342.89 with corresponding liquidated damages for the unfinished work and for
the Office of the Municipal Engineer to complete the project accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Human Resource
Management and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to discontinue the practice of
allowing deductions of monthly loan amortizations from the personnel’s payroll other
than those mandated by law. Strictly comply with the GAA provision that monthly
take home pay of employee shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the  Municipal
   Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the  Auditor,  within
   ten (10) days after receipt of the bank statements.


   We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and/or  Municipal
   Treasurer secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the bank statements after
   the end of each month for the timely preparation of the BRS.


   Furthermore, we recommended that the LCE  provide  additional  accounting
   staff to assist the Municipal Accountant to prepare the various financial
   reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:

   a. The Inventory Committee to conduct a complete  physical  inventory  of
      the   Municipality’s   properties;    and    likewise    to    require
      department/office heads to submit  lists  of  properties  existing  in
      their  respective  offices  to  the  Committee  for   validation   and
      consolidation;

   b. The Inventory Committee to prepare the inventory report and submit  to
      the Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO for reconciliation, and to
      the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;

   c. The Designated GSO and Municipal Accountant to prepare,  maintain  and
      update the property cards and PPE ledger cards  for  easy  monitoring,
      proper identification and validation of properties;

   d. The Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO to reconcile their records
      and make appropriate adjustments, if  any,  to  present  accurate  and
      reliable PPE balances in the financial statements; and

   e. The Designated GSO to issue ARE to Municipal officials  and  employees
      concerned to  cover  the  equipment  issued  for  proper  control  and
      accountability of equipment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the  Municipal
   Accountant to make the following adjustments in the Financial Statements:


   a. Transfer the  unexpended/unobligated balance of the DRRMF-MOOE and QRF
      under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books; and

   b.  Revert  the  unutilized  LDRRMF  for  CYs  2010-2013   amounting   to
      ₱4,943,401.56 to the unappropriated surplus of the  General  Fund  and
      made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by
      the Sangguniang Bayan.
   We also recommended that the  LCE  direct  the  Municipal  Disaster  Risk
   Reduction Management Officer to:


   a. Review and update the LDRRMFIP  annually to include list  of  specific
      projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of
      previous years; and


   b. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant  and  OIC-Budget  Officer  in
      regard to the utilization of  the  LDRRMF  to  ensure  reliability  of
      financial information and  proper  monitoring  of  available  disaster
      funds.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
   municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund  immediately
   their unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD
   No. 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid  accumulation
   of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair  presentation  of  the
   relevant accounts in the financial statements.


   We also recommended for the imposition of appropriate sanctions  for  the
   unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to liquidate
   their cash advances.


   We further recommended that the Municipality shall follow the  guidelines
   and procedures set in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December  19,  2016
   on the write-off, if warranted, of the unliquidated cash  advances  which
   have been long outstanding/ remained dormant in the books.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to:

   a. Review/analyze prior years’ transactions  pertaining  to  unidentified
      discrepancies,  prepare  a  reconciliation  report  and  take  up  the
      necessary  adjustments  to  correct  the  balances  of  the   affected
      accounts; and
   b. Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal  accounts  and
      effect adjustments of reconciling items  periodically,  as  identified
      and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted
      reconciling items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  OIC-Municipal
   Treasurer  to  deposit  all  collections  intact  with   the   authorized
   government depository bank daily or not later than the next  banking  day
   as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of  COA
   Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended the following:


   a. The Local Chief Executive, as Chairman, and all the other  members  of
      the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to  prepare  the
      Annual Investment Plan containing priority development projects to  be
      funded out of the 20% DF, to include only  specific  and  identifiable
      Programs/Projects/ Activities (PPAs) with  corresponding  quantifiable
      targets, attainable within the desired time frame, and consistent with
      the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1;   Moreover,  the  MPDC  and
      other heads of offices shall regularly monitor  the  progress  of  the
      projects, their timely, effective and  efficient  implementation,  and
      the commitment from the concerned offices to  implement  the  PPAs  as
      planned; and

   b. Ensure  that  the  20%  DF  is  optimally  and/or  fully  utilized  in
      accordance with the guidelines to achieve the desirable socio-economic
      development and environmental outcomes and maximize the  benefits  for
      the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE),  as  Chairperson,
   together with the other members of the LDRRM  Council,  comply  with  the
   disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage to adequately prepare
   the Municipality when hazards/disasters strike.


   We also recommended that the LCE provide additional  personnel  qualified
   to occupy the three (3) staff positions under the LDRRMO.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned
   officials to:

   a. Set-up and maintain a GAD Database that will serve as basis for gender-
      responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;

   b. Ensure timely preparation and submission of the Annual  GAD  Plan  and
      Budget and Accomplishment Report  in  required  format  together  with
      necessary documents to the DILG Regional  Office  VI  for  review  and
      approval to ascertain compliance with the guidelines; and

   c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG,
      DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive,  as  Chairperson  of  the
   Municipal Solid Waste  Management  Board,  spearhead  the  efficient  and
   effective  enforcement  of  the  Municipal   Environment   Ordinance   in
   compliance with RA No. 9003 by directing the concerned officials to:


   a. Require and/or observe strict implementation of mandatory  segregation
      of  solid  wastes  from  source  to  disposal  facility,  and   impose
      appropriate fines/ sanctions for violators;


   b.  Continuously  intensify  information,  education  and   communication
      campaign on proper waste segregation and disposal, and  the  necessity
      of the establishment of a Materials Recovery  Facility  (MRF)  to  the
      community through coordination with the officials of the barangays;

   c. Take appropriate actions to provide adequate facilities to the MRF  so
      that it will be fully operational; and


   d. Collect garbage fees from both residential and  commercial  areas  for
      Solid  Waste  Management  services  rendered,  as  provided   in   the
      Ordinance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  OIC-Municipal
   Treasurer  and  Municipal  Accountant  to  properly  monitor  the  moneys
   withheld for the BIR and GSIS, and remit intact the full amounts  to  the
   concerned government agencies within  the  required  period  pursuant  to
   Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Regulation  No.
   16-2005  and  RA  No.  8291,  otherwise  the  interests,  penalties   and
   surcharges on late remittances will be  the  personal  liability  of  the
   concerned Municipal officials.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:

   a. The Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer  to  monitor  the
      receipts and corresponding disbursements of fund transfers, ensure the
      completeness of the liquidation reports and  supporting  documents  in
      accordance with Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and submit
      the same to the DILG RO VI and the Audit Team, if such  projects  were
      already implemented;




   b. The Municipal Accountant to  verify  and  reconcile  the  differences/
      variances in the Due to NGAs, and effect the necessary adjustments  in
      the books of accounts, if any, to conform with the amounts provided by
      the DILG, and to present the correct amount of the liabilities in  the
      financial statements;

   c. The  Municipal  Engineer  to  undertake  the  procurement  process  if
      projects  had  not  been  implemented,  and  to  observe  the   agreed
      prescribed period, with strict compliance to the provisions of RA  No.
      9184 and its Revised  IRR,  as  well  as  budgeting,  accounting,  and
      auditing rules and regulations; and

   d.  The  OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  to  refund/return  to  the  DILG   the
      unexpended balance  of  the  fund  transfer  upon  completion  of  the
      project.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant and OIC-Municipal  Budget  Officer  to  observe  strictly  the
   provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS  Nos.  1  and  24,  by
   submitting the complete set of financial statements, to include the SCBAA
   and supporting disclosure of an explanation of the reasons  for  material
   differences, if any, to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  all  the  concerned
   Department Heads/Division Chiefs to submit their action plans and  status
   of implementation on the CY 2017 AAR recommendations  including  that  of
   prior years, related/connected to their  departments/divisions,  and  the
   information gathered therein be used to properly  accomplish  the  AAPSI,
   and submit the same to the COA Auditor.


   We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI  be  submitted  to
   the COA Auditor within sixty (60) days from the receipt of the AAR,
   either in printed form or by way of electronic document.
We again recommended that:
observations.managementAction: a) the General Service Officer to conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and cards to recognize the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality and the additional task of releasing the tricycle plates and business permits entailing additional time. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, page 38)
observations.reason: The RPCPPE and Property Cards submitted by the management was not adequate and the inventory report was not reflective of the total property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality due to the additional task of releasing the tricycle plates and business permits entailing additional time.

The Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provision of Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS manual in order to ensure reliability of the balances of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables; b. The Municipal Accountant to correct the recorded amount of receivables under the General Fund and Special Education Fund under JEV No. 100-2019-03-051 and 200-2019-03-011, respectively; c.
