However, the Municipal Accountant did not maintain subsidiary ledger for the current and prior years accounts as it will entail needing additional staff to do the task. The Accounting Office maintains record of Real Property Tax Payment starting CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to evaluate and reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts with the Province and furnish supporting documents such as copies of JEVs and notices of collections to enable reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the validity of the unreconciled balances and to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances. Also Municipal Accountant to request Provincial Accountant for copies of the JEVs as basis for recording the collections made by the Municipality in the books of the Province and effect adjustments if necessary.
observations.reason: There was a noted difference between the Municipal books and Provincial books for CY 2018 mainly due to unrecorded RPT payments in the books of the Province. The Municipal Accountant should furnish the Provincial Accountant copies of JEVs showing the amounts due to LGU as basis for recording. The Provincial Accountant was expected to furnish Municipal Accountant with copies of their JEVs for reconciliation. The balances remain doubtful.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to correct the two receivable accounts and observe the proper classification and recording of the transactions as provided for under COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to adjust the two receivable accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entries correcting the previous recording as per JEV No. 10-2018-08-111.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek authority from the Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus to reflect the correct balances of payables as of yearend, in pursuant to Republic Act (RA) No. 3526 implemented by Executive Order (EO) No. 109, s.1993.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer requested Sanggunian Bayan for the reversion of long outstanding payable in the amount of P1,106,179.71 and the same has been endorsed by the Office of the Mayor to the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: Only a portion of the total accounts payable for more than two years were requested for reversion. The Municipality requested reversion of P1,106,179.71 and the same was endorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan; other outstanding payables remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the collections due to the national government agencies to the Bureau of Treasury and to avoid possible misuse of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer remitted some amounts; only the amount of P128,380.44 representing unexpended balance from BUB was reverted to Treasury under JEV No. 300-2018-04-017 dated April 4, 2018.
observations.reason: Only the amount of P128,380.44 of the unexpended balance from BUB was reverted to Treasury. Other trust receipts/unexpended balances remain unremitted or unresolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and/or the Municipal Engineer to optimally utilize the 20% Local Development Fund in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents and ensure that all projects for implementation by the Municipality are included in its Annual/Supplemental Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to better plan and implement 20% DF projects; the LGU presented itemized projects in the Annual Budget for CY 2018 with specific development projects and percentages.
observations.reason: For CY 2018 projects were itemized and the LGU utilized funds for development projects. However, some projects were still appropriated in lump sum in prior periods and delays in implementation were observed. Increase of 8.81% in the non-implementation over last year was noted (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 9, page 57).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO, MPDO and Municipal Accountant to comply with COA Circular 2012-002 on preparing the LDRRMFIP, submit accurate Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, transfer unexpended balances to Trust Fund and provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements and revert balances that exceeded 5-year holding period to unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO and MPDO coordinated, and portions of the 70% Mitigation Fund-Capital Outlay were utilized. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEVs for transfers of some unexpended balances; disclosure to be incorporated in CY 2019 notes to financial statements. The LGU stated some balances were reserved as response funds and will revert amounts exceeding the 5-year validity in CY 2019.
observations.reason: 70% Mitigation Fund-CO projects were implemented (89% utilized) but the presentation in lump sum and non-inclusion of unexpended balances from prior years in the LDRRMFIP persisted. Some transfers to Trust Fund were delayed due to timing of check processing and deposit. Disclosure in Notes to FS not made for CY 2018; planned for CY 2019. Unexpended balances for 2011-2013 still in Trust Liability and not yet reverted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Executive Committee and Technical Working Group adhere to the complete structure of GFPS, ensure participation of all members in GAD trainings, formulate gender issues from GAD database and forward to the province, conduct early planning and budgeting for GAD, and assess compliance through a checklist.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred and will revisit administrative order LMA 2018-0068 and make necessary changes per MCW IRR, request official designation of TWG Chair, include MENRO and DRRMO in GFPS, and coordinate with DSWD Regional Office for GAD database training schedules.
observations.reason: Some actions taken: GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018 submitted and later endorsed by DILG (delayed). GAD Database exists (data 2015-2017) but not fully operational; GAD AR submitted on Jan 28, 2019 lacked attachments and signature of Municipal Mayor; additional improvements needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure compliance with RA 9184 procedures, validate bidding documents, and that the Municipal Accountant ensure complete supporting documents before payment.
observations.managementAction: BAC to validate documents, ensure compliance; Municipal Accountant to reclassify entries and prepare adjusting entries for SEF items recorded improperly as Buildings; Municipal Prosecutor/Local School Board to follow up. Municipal Accountant reclassified some items under JEV Nos. 200-2019-03-013, 200-2019-03-014, 200-2019-03-015 dated March 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality allocate 1% of its IRA to establish the LCPC and organize Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC) and activate and strengthen it pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: Municipality has allocated P8,500,000 of its IRA for CY 2018 to establish the Local Councils for the Protection of Children and the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC).

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen collection measures and the Municipal Mayor to require remedial actions and enforcement of tax remedies including referral to PTO for levy and public auction where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer to enforce remedial measures and submitted RPT delinquents to PTO for application of civil remedies and imposition of levy as first step of enforcement. Municipal Mayor to require intensification of collection strategies.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer sent the RPT delinquents to the Provincial Treasurer's Office (PTO) for levy application and subsequent remedies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Auction and Satellite Market Office to: a) justify necessity of continuing the operation and provide information on action taken to make the market viable; b) adopt marketing strategies to attract stallholders/investors; c) encourage possible stallholders to rent or lease spaces; d) conduct study on possible utilization of portion of land for income generating projects.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Auction and Satellite Market to promote the satellite market and undertake strategies to attract stallholders; management to adopt marketing strategies and consider opening to companies for product promotion. Plans to implement strategies and invite investors were disclosed; some promotional activities (e.g., Globe Company event) took place.
observations.reason: The Municipal Office indicated efforts to promote the market and some events were held, but full recovery measures, formal feasibility/business plans and execution of lease contracts remained outstanding; the satellite market continued to operate at a loss. Contracts and full implementation of recommended strategies not yet in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Services Office to initiate immediately the processing of insurance policies of various properties and furnish GSIS the complete inventory report for appraisal and computation of premiums.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office initiated conduct of inventory and requested GSIS to inspect insurable properties of the LGU. The GSO requested the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of records and supporting documents for processing.
observations.reason: The GSO initiated inventory and requested GSIS inspection; however, insurance premiums for some buildings/properties were not yet issued by GSIS as the Municipality could not determine the market value of the remaining properties. Some required supporting documents were not available or not turned over to the incumbent GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to conduct site inspection and prepare reports on actual usage of each parcel; the Municipal Legal Officer to execute Deed of Donation as necessary; and the Municipal Accountant to record assessed values in LGU books.
observations.managementAction: The GSO acquired some lands from the Municipal Assessor and is scheduling inspection. Legal Officer had resigned; deeds and titles require further processing. The Municipal Accountant was to submit assessed values for recording but some supporting documents were not yet provided.
observations.reason: Some lands have documents in process; Deed of Donation record could not be retrieved; legal officer resigned and action delayed; municipal assessor did not submit all necessary supporting documents for recording; titling and full transfer remain pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended review and possible rescision of the Deed of Assignment and lease agreements; require BAC to ensure bidding procedures were complied with; require Municipal Treasurer to determine unpaid RPT and take appropriate collection; require analysis of lease reasonableness per DPWH guidelines; and pursue appropriate legal action where warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government explained that the Lemery Public Market is still under private management (Primark) and that public bidding for lease was not conducted. The Municipality noted difficulties in rescinding the contract and stated they would need further legal and administrative actions. The Municipal Treasurer declared some late payments and coordinated with PTO.
observations.reason: Management indicated that they were aware and that certain actions would be difficult due to existing agreements and assignments. Some steps have been taken (e.g., requesting information and coordination with PTO) but full rescission/termination and full compliance with recommendations was not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer conduct site inspection and determine actual usage; the Municipal Assessor to retrieve required information and refer matter to concerned agencies for titling; the Municipal Accountant to recognize assessed values in LGU books.
observations.managementAction: GSO acquired the lands from the Municipal Assessor and is scheduling inspection. Some lands were provided to BSU and other agencies with funds transferred; Deed of Donation for BSU-Lemery could not be retrieved; Municipal Accountant to record assessed values pending submission.
observations.reason: Inspection of lands not yet conducted by GSO due to manpower constraints and missing records; Deed of Donation record not located; Municipal Assessor did not provide all assessed value records; titling and recording remain pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Commission on Audit for write-off of receivables if supporting documents cannot be located; otherwise exert diligent effort to locate supporting documents; collect through salary deductions if valid.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was to exert effort to locate supporting documents and employees still in service to collect through salary deductions; if effort is futile to request write-off per COA Rules of Procedure.
observations.reason: Some efforts to locate supporting documents were undertaken; however, supporting documents for some receivables remain unavailable. The process to request write-off has not been fully completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the contractor for refund of excessive construction/development cost and pursue recovery of excessive amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer coordinated and requested recovery of the excessive amount; a Notice of Disallowance was issued (Notice of Disallowance No. 17-001 (12-14) dated November 10, 2017) and LGU filed appeal memorandum received by COA Regional Office IV-A on May 18, 2018. The LGU's petition/appeal was processed.
observations.reason: The Municipality filed an appeal/memorandum which was received by COA Regional Office; collection/recovery pending resolution of the appeal. Some actions taken (Notice of Disallowance issued) but recovery not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended site inspection, prepare reports on actual usage, secure necessary documents for titling, execute Deed of Donation where applicable, and recognize assessed values in the LGU books.
observations.managementAction: GSO acquired the lands from the Municipal Assessor and scheduled inspection; some lands transferred to agencies (BSU-Lemery) with funds; Deed of Donation for BSU-Lemery could not be located; Municipal Accountant to record assessed values upon submission of documents.
observations.reason: Inspections and titling not yet completed due to missing documents and manpower constraints; Deed of Donation records missing; Municipal Assessor did not submit required documents for recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to locate supporting documents and if futile request write-off of receivables except Disallowances/Charges per COA rules.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to attempt locating documents and if not possible prepare request for write-off to COA in accordance with procedures.
observations.reason: Some efforts made to locate support but several receivables still without documentation; write-off process not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended HRMO to ensure compliance with CSC rules and policies on leaves, increments and OIC designations and to stop practices contrary to CSC rules.
observations.managementAction: Management to nullify unauthorized salary increases and seek refund where applicable; HRMO to ensure compliance and strict observance of CSC rules.
observations.reason: Some corrective actions initiated (letters sent, requests for refund), but full compliance and recovery of questioned amounts not completed as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended coordination with contractor to recover excessive amounts and to address deficiencies noted by COA.
observations.managementAction: LGU filed appeal memorandum and coordinated with COA; Notice of Disallowance was issued and LGU responded via appeal memorandum.
observations.reason: Appeal filed and under COA review; recovery and final resolution pending outcome of appeal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following: 

The Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties;

Involve each office of the Municipality to conduct respective inventory taking and tagging using stickers with control numbers and record all PPE existing in the offices and shall be validated by the Inventory Committee;

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended for the imposition of appropriate sanctions for the unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances.

We further recommended that the Municipality shall follow the guidelines and procedures set in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances which have been long outstanding/ remained dormant in the books.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs together with the supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
We further recommended that the LCE provide additional accounting staff to assist the Municipal Accountant to prepare the various financial reports.

We recommended the following:

Require and/or observe the strict implementation of mandatory segregation of solid wastes from source to disposal facility;
Organize information, education and communication campaign on proper waste segregation and disposal to the community through coordination with the officials of the barangays; and
Establish MRF that includes solid waste transfer station or sorting station, drop-off center, composting and recycling facility.

We recommended the following:

The LCE, as Chairman, and all the other members of the Municipal  Development Council (MDC) prepare the AIP containing priority development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include only specific and identifiable programs/ projects/activities (PPAs) with corresponding quantifiable targets, attainable within the desired time frame, and consistent with the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1;  Moreover, the MDC and other heads of offices shall regularly monitor the progress of the projects, their timely, effective and efficient implementation, and the commitment from the concerned offices to implement the PPAs as planned; and

Ensure that the 20%DF is optimally and/or fully utilized in accordance with the guidelines to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and maximize the benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson, together with the other members of the LDRRM Council, comply with the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage to adequately prepare the Municipality when hazards/ disasters strike.

We also recommended that the LDRRMF be fully utilized for the implementation of projects and activities contained in the LDRRMFIP for the year to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the SEF in accordance to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization for monitoring, transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with the requirements prescribed in PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and to maximize implementation of GAD related programs and activities that will address gender issues and concerns in the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE enforce the COE Nos. 2016-083 and 2016-084, by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the audit disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

The OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to monitor the receipts and corresponding disbursements of funds, ensure the completeness of the liquidation reports and supporting documents, in accordance with Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, and submit the same to the IPG and IA Audit Team, if such projects are already implemented

The OIC-Municipal Engineer to undertake the procurement process for the unimplemented projects,  and implement these within the prescribed period agreed in the MOA, with strict compliance to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, as well as budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations; and
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to refund/return to the IPG the unexpended balance of the fund transfer upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 on the timely, complete and proper submission of required reports.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairman of the MDRRMC, together with the other members of the Council, ensure that the approved priority PPAs in the LDRRMFIP are implemented as planned to improve the Municipality’s disaster resilience.  Further, we recommended that the Council and other personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-1 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees, as follows:

Prepare the GPB in accordance with the prescribed procedures to ensure that gender issues identified are the existing concerns/ problems in the Municipality, and that planned PPAs address/ respond to such gender issues. Likewise, prioritize the PPAs that address the more urgent gender issues; and
Prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with the procedures and format prescribed under JMC No. 2013-01 and support it with the necessary documents.

We recommended that the LSB prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by schedules of PPAs, and the Municipality to disburse funds in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 5447. The utilization of the SEF should focus in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Municipal officials to give priority for the implementation of allowable projects/activities included in the school board budget for the year.
We recommended that the LSB prepare the annual SEF/school board budget, supported by schedules of PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the fund in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7160.

We also recommended that the LSB focus the utilization of the SEF in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized by the Municipality according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall take the lead in mainstreaming GAD in LGU PPAs. The GFPS shall adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not established its  GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal officials strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and DBM BC No. 2014-03. They shall see to it that payments of incentives to government personnel be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing payments of such to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.
observations.reason: Settlements of  disallowances are   on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.    95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO-Designate to see to it that all purchases of gasoline, fuel and oil are supported with complete documentation, all DTTs are properly accomplished and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 77-61.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF to the Auditor not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
We recommended that the LGU officials and employees strictly adhere to the provisions of NBC No. 486, as amended.

We also recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials and employees to refund their excess claims considering the requirement that any amount in excess of the prescribed rate shall be at the expense of the participants.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 by submitting a copy of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its execution.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to strictly remit all taxes withheld from suppliers of goods and services within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made as stipulated under BIR Regulation 2-98 and Section 114 of RA No. 8424.
We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the CY 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,341,420.00.
observations.reason: Disallowances are partially settled by employees thru direct payments to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties, prepare the inventory reports of all PPE and submit said reports to the Auditor, Municipal Accountant and GSO/ Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Inventory Reports of all PPE are not submitted by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the inventory reports be reconciled with the accounting records in order to present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statement. Further, the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer prepare and update the PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for proper identification of all PPE.
observations.reason: Inventory Reports were not updated nor reconciled with the records of the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Utilization of DRRMF to the Auditor within the reglementary period using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Monthly reports for the Utilization of DRRMF were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations
We also recommended that properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted within 60 days from receipt of the AAR to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no sufficient budget/ fund to establish a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2014 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.  

We also recommended that the LCE shall henceforth, submit the properly accomplished AAPSI within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the AAR to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the Real Property Tax Registers or Individual Taxpayer’s Index Cards  duly  certified  by  the Treasurer as basis in setting-up SET Receivables at the beginning of the year to ensure accuracy and to avoid misstatement of the SET Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the concerned municipal employees to prepare a Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which contains the list of projects
and activities to be funded from prior years’ balance of the unexpected QRF and
DRRMF-MOOE;
Require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to
submit the Monthly Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to make a full disclosure on the details of the year-end
fund balance in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
No action yet. The MDRRMO and MPDO had not yet prepared the LDRRMFIP in
detailed specific projects during the year.
The LDRRMO prepared the monthly source and utilization however, was not submitted on
a timely manner to the Audit Team.
Accounting staff commented that disclosure were not fully provided in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason:
The recommendation was not complied with during CY 2017 due to non-compliance with
the requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Market Administrator to
adopt marketing strategies to encourage possible stallholders or investors to rent or lease
spaces available in the satellite market in order to establish business activity in the locality
and to generate the expected rental income thereat.
Likewise, to conduct study on the possible utilization of the portion of the land purchased
two years ago for income generating project.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Auction and Satellite Market had exerted effort to invite stallholders and
investors to occupy the vacant stalls however no applications or interest had been
submitted due to inaccessibility and location of the market. For CY 2018, the Municipal
Government will adopt strategies to encourage investors to put up their businesses in the
satellite market.
observations.reason:
The satellite market had not achieved the expected income due to the failure of the
management to operate the market to its fullest.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other
concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented
during the year, in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer admitted that they failed to submit the report within the
prescribed period and assured the Audit Team that they will submit the reports at the
beginning of the year in the succeeding years.
observations.reason: This observation was reiterated in CY 2017 due to late submission of PPA reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
coordinate with the contractor for the refund of P825,948.78 on the cost of deficiency noted
by the COA Engineers to recover the excessive amount on the construction/development
of the sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer had already notified the contractor to return the excess amount.
However, there was an appeal filed with the Regional Office.
observations.reason: The contractor filed an appeal with the Regional Office; refund not yet recovered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and to
prepare and submit financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and
Sections 107 to 110 of the NGAs Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant had not prepared and submitted the subsidiary accounts and
financial statements for the special accounts. The recommendation was reiterated in CY
2017.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant had not prepared and submitted required financial statements
and subsidiary ledgers for special accounts in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

   a. The Inventory Committee to conduct a complete  physical  inventory  of
      the Municipality’s properties;

   b. Involve each office/unit of the  Municipality  to  conduct  respective
      inventory taking and tagging using stickers with control  numbers  and
      record all PPE existing in the offices and same shall be validated  by
      the Inventory Committee;

   c. Consolidate  all  validated  inventory  reports  from  the  respective
      offices, together with  the  corresponding   property  numbers,  which
      shall be used as basis for the preparation of the PPE ledger cards and
      Inventory Report to be submitted by the  Inventory  Committee  to  the
      Auditor, Municipal Accountant and GSO for proper identification of all
      PPE;

   d. The Accounting and General Services Offices to reconcile their records
      and any discrepancies be adjusted to  properly  present  accurate  and
      reliable PPE balances in the financial statements; and

   e. Prepare an updated ARE,  with  corresponding  serial  numbers,  to  be
      issued to concerned accountable officers/property holders.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials  and
   employees to  liquidate  and/or  refund  immediately  their  unused  cash
   advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA
   Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid  accumulation  of  unliquidated
   cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant  accounts
   in the financial statements.


   We also recommended for the imposition of appropriate sanctions  for  the
   unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to liquidate
   their cash advances.
   We further recommended that the Municipality shall follow the  guidelines
   and procedures set in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December  19,  2016
   on the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances which have  been  long
   outstanding/ remained dormant in the books.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
   Treasurer to submit  all  the  paid  DVs  together  with  the  supporting
   documents to the COA Auditor and to  adhere  strictly  to  the  pertinent
   provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal  Accountant  to  prepare

    a.   Prepare   the   GPB   in   accordance    with    the    prescribed
       procedures/processes to ensure that gender issues identified are the
       existing concerns/problems in the  Municipality,  and  that  planned
       PPAs address/respond to such gender issues. Likewise, prioritize the
       PPAs that address the more urgent gender issues.
    b. Prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance  with
       the procedures and format  prescribed  under  JMC  No.  2013-01  and
       support it with the necessary documents.
    c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No.  2013-01  of  the  PCW,
       DILG, DBM and NEDA.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the LSB prepare the  Annual  School  Board  Budget,
    supported by PPAs, and the Municipality to disburse funds in accordance
    with the provisions of RA No. 5447. The utilization of the  SEF  should
    focus in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of
    quality education by public elementary and  secondary  schools  in  the
    Municipality.


    We also  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  concerned  Municipal
    officials  to  give  priority  for  the  implementation  of   allowable
    projects/activities included in the school board budget for the year.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the LCE  direct  the  OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  and
    Municipal Accountant to release the shares of the  barangays  from  the
    proceeds  of  the  basic  RPT,  including  the  interest  thereon,  and
    collections from the CTC within the prescribed period which is five (5)
    days after the end of each quarter, pursuant to Section 271 of  RA  No.
    7160. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to  it
    that the paid DVs are supported with summary/  details  of  the  amount
    released to the barangays.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned  Department  Heads
    to submit their action plans and status of  implementation  on  the  CY
    2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that  of  prior  years,
    related/connected to their departments  and  the  information  gathered
    therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit  the  same
    to the COA Auditor.


    We also recommended  that  properly  accomplished  AAPSI  be  submitted
    within 60 days from receipt of the AAR to the COA,  either  in  printed
    form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the Municipality Mayor: require the Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the conduct of physical inventory and reconciliation of balances of the account; instruct the General Service Officer to conduct complete inventory of all the Municipal properties and submit the Inventory Report thereon on or before January 31, of the following year, inquire the status/condition/whereabouts of the properties which were not found during physical count and make an immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and other existing rules and regulations; and create an Appraisal Committee to evaluate and determine the current value/carrying value of prior years’ acquisitions without available records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already maintained a complete and updated Equipment Ledger Card. The General Services Officer conducted an inventory of municipal properties and submitted the report on February 21, 2017, however, this pertained only to machinery and equipment and not covered all PPE accounts. Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution No. 16-2491 dated December 9, 2016 disclosed that a Disposal/Appraisal Committee was already created and a meeting was conducted on September 13, 2016.
observations.reason: No records were turned over by the former GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the Modified Accrual Method of Accounting of RPT and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers with the amount due and collectible from the Municipal Treasurer; and the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a complete list of delinquent taxpayers at the end of the year for reconciliation of records to reflect reliable account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is compliant in the setting up of the Real Property and Special Tax Receivables. On December 29, 2016, the Municipal Accountant made an adjustment to reconcile the balance with the List of Delinquent Taxpayers by debiting Deferred Real Property Tax and crediting Real Property Receivables. The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal the list of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2015.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower hinders the preparation of complete and updated list of delinquent taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the annual physical count of all inventories/supplies and prepare a report thereon for submission to the local chief executive, copy furnished the Auditor concerned and instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain ledger cards and reconcile the same with stock cards of GSO as required under Section 156 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management and Section 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The General Services submitted a report on the physical count of inventories and suppliers. However, the Supplies Ledger and Stock Cards were not presented to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert diligent effort in locating the documents supporting the recorded receivables. If the receivables from employees who are still in the service are proven to be valid, collection of the same through salary deduction should be made. However, if the effort to locate the supporting documents proved futile, request for write-off of these receivables except for Disallowances/Charges, in accordance with the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit in order to rid the books of unreliable accounts, and for fair presentation in the financial statement of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: LGU was able to collect P42,781.20 for Disallowances in Trust Fund and P20.00 for Due From Officers and Employees in General Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to give a satisfactory explanation on the non preparation for the request for write off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: reconcile General Ledger-CIB balance against cashbook for LBP HYSA No. 2041-0805-42 and PNB Saving Account No. 433-531056-5. Prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement for all accounts as required under existing regulations and effect the necessary adjustments where necessary, to reflect reliable account balances; prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books for the stale checks totaling P53,498.60 for LBP AN 2042-1000-24 which were still in the books as of year-end and the under recorded interest income and deposits of P7.74 and P.30 for LBP AN 2041-0805-42 and 2042-1023-37, respectively; and record the bank service charges and debit memo for LBP CA No. 2042-1030-40 for the 1st and 2nd quarter of CY 2015 totaling P1,200.00 and P360.00 respectively.
observations.managementAction: The two balances are now reconciled. Bank Reconciliation Statements are now prepared regularly. Adjusting journal entries has been made as per JEV No. 100-16-08-122 dated August 1, 2016. The amount of P1,200.00 was recorded per JEV No. 300-16-07-013 dated July 1, 2016 and P360.00 was taken up per JEV No. 300-16-12-024 dated December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: Require the BAC/TWG to submit documents/computation on which they relied on in declaring PAPISSS Inc. as financially eligible and to meticulously check/evaluate the veracity of the documents submitted by the bidders; Require the Municipal Accountant to draw the adjusting entries in the General Fund by debiting Markets and crediting Government Equity accounts by P6,561,078.90; and Require the OIC-Municipal Engineer to submit the required lacking documents and explanation on the deficiencies noted by the audit team in the documentation and deviation in the contract time and actual number of days the project was completed.
observations.managementAction: The BAC are now checking and evaluating the completeness and correctness submitted by interested bidders. Recorded per JEV No. 100-16-01-011 dated January 29, 2016. Some required documents were already submitted.
observations.reason: The submitted documents are subject for further evaluation by the team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to identify the causes of the unreconciled balance between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustment to present a reliable financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised that reciprocal accounts are reconciled at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: In entering agreement, all provisions stipulated therein must be complied with to avoid similar problems in the future; Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the bidding documents were already prepared which include an Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) before starting the procurement process; Ensure that all contracts signed be supported with certificate of availability of fund signed by the Municipal Treasurer; and Require the Municipal Engineer to check the accuracy of the Program of Works being prepared and to assess/evaluate the significant deviations made by the contractors.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality’s counterpart of 10% for the SALINTUBIG project was not provided. Approved Budget for the Contract was now prepared before the start of the procurement process. Some required documents were already submitted. Re: Ensure that all contracts signed be supported with certificate of availability of fund was fully implemented.
observations.reason: No available appropriation of which the 10% counterpart can be charged.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee: ensure that pertinent provision of IRR of RA 9184 are strictly adhered to promote transparency and economy of its procurement process; Ensure that Invitation to Bid (ITB), Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Approved Contract be posted in PhilGEPs and conspicuous places to draw more interested/prospective bidders that would result to a competitive bidding to attain top quality and reasonable price to the advantage/benefit of the Municipality as well as promote transparency and full disclosure; Verify the computation of Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC) of the contractor. Require the contractors the submission of accurate Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC) not only to include ongoing and awarded but not yet started projects of the contractor with the Municipality but also outside the Municipality whether it is private or public project; and Refrain from entering into contract with the contractors if it is after 10 days from the receipt of the notice of award.
observations.managementAction: The BAC ensured that they strictly observed the rules and regulations on procurement. Some print outs of the PhilGEPS posting were submitted for validation. The Bids and Award Committee checked the computation of NFCC as required by existing rules and regulations. The Bids and Award Committee see to it that the Notice of Award is received within the required period by the winning contractor/supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Concerned officials of the LGU to fully utilize the 70 percent Mitigation for the implementation of various disaster preparedness programs/projects for the benefits of its constituents; Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the requirements set forth in the DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 6 and COA Circ. No. 2012-001. Ensure that all disbursements charged against calamity fund be in accordance with the said circular be supported with all the required documents. Refrain from paying disbursement vouchers with incomplete supporting documents; Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit to the Audit Team the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF for timely audit and verification purposes; and Municipal Accountant to transfer and record the transferred amount by year the balance of 30% QRF and 70% unused MOOE to the Special Trust Liability account under Trust Liability-DRRM under code 438.
observations.managementAction: There were balances for 70% Mitigation fund of P1,021,238.89 for Capital Outlay and P731,903.49 for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses. Municipal Accountant to transfer and record the transferred amount by year the balance of 30% QRF and 70% unused MOOE to the Special Trust Liability account under Trust Liability-DRRM was transferred to Trust Fund per JEV No. 100-16-12-207 dated December 29, 2016 the amount of P3,830,536.50.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO was not aware of this report however, he promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials implement the programs/projects to the trust embodied in EO No. 43 for the development of its constituents particularly the marginalized sector.
observations.managementAction: Funds received in CY 2015 for the implementation of BUB projects were already implemented.

observations.recommendation: In order to effectively address the gender issues in the Municipality, we recommended that the: Municipal Mayor ensures that the composition of GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group was in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 and furnish its members with Administrative Order No.2014-0053 to inform them of their additional duty; GAD Focal Point System and its Technical Working Group provide technical assistance to the Local Planning and Development Office (LPDO) in establishing and maintaining GAD Database and ensure that it is operational; and include in the yearly programs, projects and activities the provision for the comprehensive health services of victims of violence against women to ensure that the gender issues were fully addressed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has issued Administrative Order No. LMA 2016-0010 dated 2nd day of September 2016 to update-creation of Municipal Gender and Development Focal Point System. Duties and function of the GFPS are enumerated in the abovementioned Administrative Order. Provision for the comprehensive health services of violence against women is indicated in the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board consider the programs/projects enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001, No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and No, 01 dated April 14, 1998 in the preparation of the SEF Budget and ensures that programs, projects and activities indicated in the budget were specifically identified in order to achieve the intended purpose of the fund. Henceforth, the Local School Board see to it that SEF is utilized for purposes specifically identified in the established rules and regulations to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established is met.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board ensured that programs, projects and activities were enumerated in the said joint circulars were considered in the preparation of the SEF Budget. Moreover, payment of medals for distribution to public schools was now charged in the General Fund. The LSB made it a point that established rules and regulations are met.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the: Municipal Licensing Officer to require the taxpayers to submit an audited FS together with the books of accounts and other pertinent records and information for the determination of gross receipts and to maintain permanent file/audited FS of each business establishments; and Municipal Treasurer verify/assess the gross receipts of five contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling P330,035.79, and require collection thereof. The absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.
observations.managementAction: The total amount of P337,626.73 (Annex V) was collected from the concerned taxpayers mentioned in 2015 ARR.
observations.reason: Audited Financial Statements were not yet available during payment of business hence, they relied on the data given by the taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to: strictly monitor the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program in order to attain the desired results of the program so as to eliminate the number of underweight and overweight children; explain the reason for the discrepancies between the purchase goods and the distribution to the 45-day care centers as reported in the Requisition and Issue Slip and refund the amount of P1,875.50; and ensure that the cash advance will be used for purchase of goods which were not yet distributed/consumed and be supported with the estimate of expenses in which the cash advance will be used. Also ensure proper distribution goods for the subsequent feeding program of the Municipality for effective and efficient implementation of the program.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor commented that there are still cases of malnourished children in the municipality. The Office of the MSWD ensured that goods were distributed properly. The MSWDO personnel claimed that strict monitoring of goods purchased and distributed was employed. The MSWDO ensured that all cash advances granted to her was expended and supported with the necessary documents.
observations.reason: The concerned employees failed to give satisfactory explanation regarding this matter.

observations.recommendation: We again recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for special accounts and to prepare and submit financial statements as required in Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant claimed that she prepared financial statements for some special accounts in the General Fund. However, these were not yet submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Market  was leased by a private entity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of PPE, prepare the inventory reports of all offices/ departments and submit the corresponding reports to the Auditor, the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The department heads assigned personnel to conduct physical inventory of all existing/ serviceable properties in their respective offices
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund the unused cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 96-004, 97-002 and 2012-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Most of the cash advances granted in CY 2016 were liquidated within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that no additional cash advances shall be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated as provided under the above-cited provisions of law and regulations of COA.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman of the Municipal Planning and Development Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF.  

We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized according to the guidelines in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.
We recommended that the LSB prepare the annual SEF/school board budget, supported by schedules of PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the fund in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: There are still expenses that are not among the allowable expenditures prescribed in the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LSB focus the utilization of the SEF in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF.

We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized by the Municipality according to the guidelines in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC–Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall take the lead in mainstreaming GAD in LGU PPAs. The GFPS shall adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has prepared an Executive Order creating the GFPS but was pending for the approval of the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to adhere strictly to the provisions of EO No. 298 regarding claims on travel expenses.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials to refund the excess amount in the allowed claims for travel expenses.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the BS as an alternative procedure in preparing the BRS on time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the BRS but not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and/or OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006, by submitting the financial reports/accounts together with all the supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the financial reports but not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned LGU officials strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and DBM BC No. 2014-03. They shall see to it that payments of incentives to government personnel be based on laws, rules and regulations authorizing payments of such to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.
observations.managementAction: There were refunds made but only those who separated from the service settled in full, while others are paying partially direct to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.    95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO-Designate to see to it that all purchases of gasoline, fuel and oil are supported with complete documentation, all DTTs are properly accomplished and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 77-61.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare and submit/ furnish copy of the monthly reports in the prescribed forms required in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 to the NDRRMC through the Office of the Civil Defense and to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Only the 4th quarter Report on the Utilization of DRRMF was submitted by the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF to the Auditor not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: CRAAF for the year was submitted to the Auditor on March 29, 2017

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the collectors and other accountable officers to submit their individual RAAF at the end of the month as basis for the preparation and submission of the CRAAF.
We recommended that the LGU officials and employees strictly adhere to the provisions of NBC No. 486, as amended.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 by submitting a copy of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its execution.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to strictly remit all taxes withheld from suppliers of goods and services within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made as stipulated under BIR Regulation 2-98 and Section 114 of RA No. 8424.
We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the CY 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,341,420.00.
observations.managementAction: There were refunds made but only those who separated from the service settled in full, while others are paying partially direct to the Municipal Treasurer.
